Further, it provides logistics/transport equipment, including overhead cranes, belt/roller conveyors, conveyors for FPD, automatic guided vehicles, roll box pallets, mobile racks, air balancers, gear motors, and steel racks; and general industrial equipment, such as electric screwdrivers, handy scanners/terminals, spring balancers/cable reels, vacuum packaging machines, aluminum profile systems, laser welders/markers, compressors, air dryers, and chiller/temperature control equipment. The company was founded in 1906 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nitto Kohki Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, develops, manufactures, and sells couplings, machine tools, pumps, and door closers in Japan, America, Europe, East Asia, Southeast Asia, rest of Asia, and Oceania. It operates in four segments: Quick Connect Couplings Business; Power & Machine Tools Business; Pumps & Compressors Business; and Door Closers Business. The company offers quick connect couplings under the CUPLA brand, including HHV, compact zerospill, full blow, hygienic, mutli, semicon, hi, and micro for various air, water, oil, and chemical solutions; power and machine tools, such as atra ace, selfer, belton, and jet broach and chisel for boring, cutting, grinding, and scaling of steel materials; septic tank blowers and miniature labor-saving pumps for health and medical devices; and auto hinge center hang and flag type door closers, as well as sliding door closers for shopping centers, medical facilities, transportation equipment, and offices. It also provides electric screwdrivers under the delvo brand for assembling electronic devices comprising PCs, smartphones, printers, and automotive components; medo blowers; and robot products for deburring, scaling and chipping, surface sanding, screw fastening, air blow/vacuum lifting, and piping connection and disconnection. Nitto Kohki Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1951 and is headquartered in Ota, Japan.

Marubun Corporation distributes electronics products in Japan and internationally. The company offers semiconductors and electronic components, including amplifiers, clocks, wired and radio interfaces, isolation products, microcomputer processors, motor drivers, power supplies, RF and microwave, sensors, ASIC/FPGA/PLDs, memories, displays, ASSP, discrete products, protective elements, passive components, connector switches, microcomputers, analog and memory ICs, wireless solutions, circuit protection, and power control components. It also provides software products, such as cloud, enterprise software, embedded software, security, and OS/development tools; measuring equipment, tester monitoring, display/DLP, and others; inspection and analysis equipment, and other products; ICT solutions, including network and storage devices, simulator tester, server, service, security, and others; and assembly and robotics comprising assembly and mounting equipment, industrial robots, service robots, and others. In addition, the company offers laser and optical parts, such as semiconductor laser, LED/light lamp, and others; embedded computer products, including CPU board, embedded system, and others; and other devices comprising IP/license, embedded module, driving simulator, and solar/photovoltaic system. Further, it provides medical devices; and development sourcing, seminars, inspection, and maintenance services. Marubun Corporation was founded in 1844 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Icom Incorporated, together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells telecommunication equipment in Japan, the United States, Canada, Germany, Spain, Australia, China, and Vietnam. It offers land mobile radio, amateur radio, marine radio, navigation products, wireless LAN, SIP telephone equipment, aviation radio, and communications receivers, as well as information and telecommunication equipment, as well as telecommunication, warranty, repair, and maintenance services. The company was founded in 1954 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Iwatsuka Confectionery Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells rice crackers in Japan. The company offers black bean and rice crackers under Iwatsuka brand name; country rice crackers; taste savory; THE pinch; Osodefuri bean mochi; fluffy; kinako mochi; banzai sansho pepper; and Niigata wet rice crackers.It also provides sweets made from rice and boxed rice crackers/gifts. The company was formerly known as Iwatsuka Nosan Kakojo Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Iwatsuka Confectionery Co., Ltd. in 1960. Iwatsuka Confectionery Co., Ltd. was founded in 1947 and is headquartered in Nagaoka, Japan.

Shinko Shoji Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the import, export, and sale of electronic components in Japan, rest of Asia, North America, and Europe. It operates through Electronic Device Business, Assembly Business, and Other Businesses segments. The company offers semiconductors, general electronic parts and batteries, display devices, embedded computing, and solution services, as well as integrated circuits, assembly products, and other electronic equipment. It also provides in-vehicle semiconductors and passive components, including MCU, SoC, analog, power discrete, and connectors; integrated cockpit, EV, brushless DC motor control, and data logger solutions; sensing, vision, AI, and connectivity products; gaming and casino equipment; ICT on-site solutions; edge computer controllers; design and development tools; communications and networks; and non-contact services. In addition, the company offers scheme and support systems; non-Japanese printed circuit boards and harnesses; express prototyping and procurement support services; lithium-ion and battery solutions; power generation; support services for overseas transfers and electronics manufacturing services; and design and development solutions, such as CAN+A/D data loggers, model-based development, embedded software, ASIC/FPGA, and AUTOSAR. Further, it provides workstations, supercomputers, and peripheral equipment; and engages in the contract development of microcomputer software and software development activities. The company serves the automotive, industry, amusement, AI/DX, EMS/ODM, energy, design development, logistics, and office automation markets. Shinko Shoji Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1953 and is headquartered in Shinagawa, Japan.

AuBEX CORPORATION manufactures and sells nibs and medical equipment in Japan and internationally. The company offers nibs made from chemical fibers, plastics, and other material; medical equipment and components, such as pressure infusion devices and angiography guide wires; and nibs for makeup and cosmetics products. The company was formerly known as Tokyo hat Co., Ltd. and changed its name to AuBEX CORPORATION in 1985. AuBEX CORPORATION was founded in 1892 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sugimoto & Co., Ltd. engages in the sale, import, and export of machinery and apparatus in Japan and internationally. The company also offers measuring instruments; tester cutting tools; machine tools; pneumatic tools; hydraulic/pneumatic equipment; electric tools; power transmission parts; work tools; pipework equipment; and OA equipment. In addition, it provides environmental and surveying instruments; conduction and drive equipment; plumbing equipment; electrical and electronic components; environmental and physical chemistry products; packing materials; optical and scientific instruments; work machinery and equipment; tools; transportation, logistics, cargo handling, and storage equipment; grinding wheels and rivets; chemical products; safety equipment and protective gear; and housing, office automation, and research supplies. Sugimoto & Co., Ltd. was founded in 1921 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Artiza Networks, Inc. provides radio access network and core network node testing products to mobile network operators, network equipment manufacturers, system integrators, and other types of companies in the telecommunications industry. It provides gNodeB and eNodeB testing products, including Duo SIM-advanced functional and load testers, environmental simulators, QoE optimization, packet capture tools, and board related products. The company also offers functional testing, performance testing, product demonstration, and 5G solutions. In addition, it provides centralized RAN, carrier aggregation, Internet of Things, and quality assurance solutions. The company was formerly known as Able Communication Inc. and changed its name to Artiza Networks, Inc. in 2001. Artiza Networks, Inc. was incorporated in 1990 and is headquartered in Tachikawa, Japan.

Kaizen Platform, Inc., provides user experience (UX) solutions to improve conversion rates (CVR) for client websites. The company operates in two segments, Growth and Transformation. The Growth segment offers services such as, website production, customer relationship management (CRM) improvement, and video production, primarily under performance-based quasi-entrustment contracts. The Transformation segment provides consulting services for corporate digital transformation (DX), including DX workshops and system engineering services, mainly under performance-proportion quasi-commission contracts. It offers KAIZEN AD/VIDEO, which enhances advertising and mass production resources; KAIZEN CONSULTING, which supports DX project launch, strategy planning, and administrative support; KAIZEN SCHOOL, a video advertising creator training course; KAIZEN ENGINE, which improves customer experience across systems; and KAIZEN AI CLOUD, which manages various generative AI APIs and middleware. The company was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Tenryu Saw Mfg. Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells saw blades in Japan and internationally. The company offers cutting tools, such as carbide tools for ferrous and non-ferrous metals, as well as wood applications; cutters and routers; HSS tools; and diamond saw blades, circular and band saw blades, circular knives, and miter saw blades. It also offers machines for carbide-tipped saw blades, including an ultrasonic bath for saw blades; and band saw processing machines comprising automatic band saw welders, levelers, heat-tensioning machines, hard-facing alloy tipping machines, hard-facing alloy rough grinders, side grinders, and saw sharpeners, as well as narrow band saw sharpeners. Tenryu Saw Mfg. Co., Ltd. was founded in 1909 and is headquartered in Fukuroi, Japan.

Waida Mfg. Co.,Ltd. operates in the mold-related and the cutting tool-related industry in Japan, China, rest of Asia, Africa, Europe, and the United States. It offers various grinders, including profile, jig, grooving insert, digital profile, wafer surface, and milling tool grinders, as well as HAAS Multigrind grinding centers; and automatic chart marking systems. The company was founded in 1933 and is headquartered in Takayama, Japan.

Cosmo Bio Company,Limited, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a life science trading company worldwide. It is involved in the import, export, manufacture, marketing, sales, and customer support of life science research bio tools, such as reagents, kits, instruments, and software. The company also distributes antibodies, antigens, biologically active substances, enzymes, substrates, peptides, amino acids, chemicals, carbohydrates, lipids, virus components, and bacteria, as well as detection reagents, cell and tissue culture systems, and gene analysis systems; and research instruments, including electrophoresis apparatuses, cell culture labware, gene manipulation systems, and data analysis software. In addition, it provides drug discovery and custom research services; clinical diagnostics; cell and DNA, and protein engineering solutions; disposable items and general labware; and bioactive substances; consigned services, such as intestinal flora analysis, tear fluid analysis, and cell assays services; and custom peptide synthesis and antibody production services. Further, the company manufactures and sells cell/cell culture products comprising adipose tissue, bone, cartilage, tooth, pancreas, liver function, cardiovascular, immune, mesenchymal stem cells, feeder cells, tumor cell lines, macrophage-related, and coating reagents; measurement and staining kits, detection/purification kits related to neurodementia, macrophage/immune, exosome, and anti-saccharification/anti-aging; and biotechnology research equipment, such as constant-temperature transport containers, aluminum block mat heat insulation devices, and dew condensation prevention glass plate. It serves universities, public research institutes, and companies engaged in biological science. Cosmo Bio Company, Limited was founded in 1978 and is headquartered in Koto, Japan.

Okayama Paper Industries Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of paper products in Japan and internationally. It operates through Paperboard Business and Decorative Corrugated Board Business segments. The company provides core paper, paper tube base paper, paper boards, flat board, packaging, gift boxes, and decorative cardboards; cosmetic cardboard; and digital high speed printing services. In addition, its products are used for winding paper, cloth, cellophane, tape, thread, fruits and vegetables, food, home appliances, and pharmaceuticals. Okayama Paper Industries Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1907 and is headquartered in Okayama, Japan.

Furubayashi Shiko Co.,Ltd. plans, designs, prints, processes, manufactures, and sells packaging products in Japan, China, and Taiwan. The company offers gift boxes for gifts, special moisture-proof papers, paper tubes, carrier cartons, decorative corrugated cardboard boxes, bag-in-cartons, system cartons, etc., and printed paper for packing food, beverages, confectionery, pharmaceuticals, soaps, detergents, cosmetics, miscellaneous goods, electrical equipment, and agricultural products. It provides packaging materials through cellophane, resin film, metal foil, laminate film, etc. for liquids, solids, powders, etc., as well as bags, standing pouches, labels, etc. In addition, the company offers laminations comprising paperboards, papers, resin films, metal foils, and barrier coatings; molded containers, which include synthetic resin containers, synthetic resins, composite containers, and paperboards, super cold containers, and heat-resistant containers, etc.; and commercial print products, such flyers, catalogs, posters, POP, store displays, etc. Further, it provides filling, packaging, and multi-pack packaging machines for bottles, cans, etc.; labeling systems and labelers; vertical and horizontal cartoning machines and stacking packaging machines; and packaging line streamlining and engineering services, as well as contract packaging services for confectionery, food, cosmetics, miscellaneous goods, etc. Furubayashi Shiko Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1934 and is based in Osaka, Japan.

Asahi Kagaku Kogyo Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells plastic molded products and molding dies in Japan. It engages in resin molding of power tools and auto parts; assembly of power tools; design and production of resin molds; and manufacturing and sales of construction materials. The company also provides ALC anchor, plasterboard anchors, concrete anchors, earth anchors, and power tools, auto parts, and other parts. Asahi Kagaku Kogyo Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1962 and is headquartered in Anjo, Japan.

UNITED, Inc. engages in the ad-technology, content, and investment businesses in Japan. It offers Bypass, an Ad distribution platform for agencies and advertisers; adstir, Ad management platform for publishers; Crash Fever, a role-playing game; and CocoPPa Play, a dress-up avatar service. The company also operates CHEERZ, a service to help invigorate artists' activities; plans, develops, and operates TechAcademy, a Japan's online programming school; produces sports-related websites; plans and develops media; plans and operates Dream Mail, a sweepstakes and gift site; and provides consulting solutions to support the digital transformation of companies, as well as develops various applications. In addition, it is involved in the venture capital investment business. UNITED, Inc. was incorporated in 1998 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Ichi Software, Inc. develops and sells computer software in Japan, Asia, North America, Europe, and other countries. It operates through Entertainment Business, and Student Dormitories and Other Businesses segments. The company offers home video game software; and game content for mobile phones and smartphones. It also manages student dormitories; and invests in real estate properties. The company was incorporated in 1995 and is headquartered in Kakamigahara, Japan.

Havix Corporation engages in the non-woven fabric and paper related businesses in Japan. It offers pulp nonwoven fabrics; synthetic fiber nonwoven fabrics; and sanitary paper, such as disposable diapers and wet towels. The company was incorporated in 1950 and is headquartered in Gifu, Japan.

Solekia Limited handles electronic devices, semiconductors, component/device solutions, system integration, and other IT solutions in Japan and internationally. The company sells semiconductor and component products, embedded devices, monitors, and storage equipment. It also engages in software development, system integration, and network solution services, as well as consulting, construction, operation, and maintenance; and IT infrastructure design, construction, system monitoring, help desk, BCP drafting, and disaster recovery program. In addition, the company is involved in the IT Solution business activities such as ERP, BCP, environment, and energy-saving, corresponding to the needs of government municipal offices and local government authorities, manufacturing, distribution, agricultural cooperatives, consumers' cooperative association, financial operations, and healthcare industries. The company was formerly known as Kobayashi Denshi Sangyo Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Solekia Limited in April 2002. Solekia Limited was incorporated in 1958 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Kyoritsu Air Tech Inc., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells building and residential equipment in Japan. The company offers building air conditioning and disaster prevention equipment, such as dampers, air outlets, and fasteners; residential central air conditioning systems; ventilation systems; air outlets and VAV/Fass units; wall-mounted heat exchange air purifiers; radiant cooling/heating systems; exhaust system for induction cookers; water purifiers; and air conditioning materials, as well as ductless booster circulation fan. It also provides low-carbon eco-materials and commercial kitchen hoods. Kyoritsu Air Tech Inc. was founded in 1967 and is based in Kasuya, Japan.

Kikusui Chemical Industries Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of functional, architectural finishing, construction, and civil engineering materials primarily in Japan. The company offers exterior coating materials, exterior architectural finishing coating materials, and other coating materials; primer and anti-rust paints; structural renovation products; stripping agents; fire-resistant and heat insulating materials; tile-related and auxiliary materials; civil engineering related products; roofing and waterproofing products; interior and asbestos countermeasure related products; and steel frame/iron parts. It also provides various machinery and equipment. The company was incorporated in 1959 and is based in Nagoya, Japan.

TYK Corporation engages in the manufacture and sale of functional refractories and ceramics worldwide. It offers converter, pig iron ladles, secondary refining, gas blowing, large precast blocks, lance pipes, blast furnace, continuous casting, cement kiln and lime kiln, incinerator and industrial furnace, and auxiliary machinery and equipment. The company also provides fine ceramics, such as alumina, magnesia, zirconia, and silicon nitride and carbide; die-cast sleeves; graphite sheet, carbon tray, and special carbon materials; and NOTORP continuous copper sensor, NOTORP-G, and NOTORP-GII devices for iron and steelmaking, cast iron/nonferrous metal, chemistry, electrical and electronic equipment, car, food processing and agriculture, gas, and medical care industries. In addition, it offers engineering services; and analysis products, including atomic absorption photometer, infrared spectrophotometer, thermal balance, particle size distribution meter, multi-specimen specific surface area/pore size distribution measuring device, carbon/sulfur analyzer, scanning electron microscope, fluorescence X-ray device, X-ray diffractometer, and thermal expansion measuring device. TYK Corporation was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

JDC Corporation engages in the construction business in Japan and rest of Asia. It operates through Civil Engineering Business, Construction Business, and Construction in Progress-related Business segments. The company is involved in the construction of dams, bridges, tunnels, roads, water supply and sewerage, landfills, stadiums, office buildings, apartments, and logistics facilities; and architectural design and business planning, building value regeneration, and food factory and seismic isolation engineering solutions. It also engages in the manufacture, sale, repair, and rental of construction and mining machinery; provision of coal ash mixed materials, refuse concrete, acetic acid absorbents, and adhesives; and operation of agricultural solar power plants and cemeteries. In addition, the company is involved in the development of electric power, transportation, ports and harbors, afforestation, flood control, irrigation, land reclamation, underground resources, and land; design, contractual, and management services related to civil engineering and building construction; provision of construction and stone materials, and chemical products; development, sale, rental, brokerage, management, and appraisal of real estate; investment, trading, brokerage, and management of investment equity; investment advisory; land and marine transportation and warehousing; quarrying; ownership, rental, and management of golf courses and ski resorts; travel agency; and environmental conservation activities, including soil and water remediation, as well as the collection, transport, processing, disposal, and recycling of municipal, industrial, and construction waste. Further, it engages in the provision of agricultural products and consulting services; generation and supply of electricity and heat; worker dispatching; and monetary loans, financial guarantees, factoring, and general leasing activities. JDC Corporation was incorporated in 1951 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Ohmoto Gumi Co., Ltd. offers construction and civil engineering services in Japan. The company undertakes civil engineering, building construction, carpentry, plastering, scaffolding and earthwork, masonry, roofing, electrical, plumbing, tile/brick/block, steel structure, stonework, reinforcement, paving, sheet metal, glass, painting, waterproofing, interior finishing, fittings, landscaping, water supply facility, and demolition works. It is also involved in dredging, landfill, real estate development, and technology development, as well as architectural and civil engineering consulting business. The company was founded in 1907 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Estelle Holdings Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells jewelry in Japan. The company offers pearls and gemstones; and eyeglass frames. It also engages in food and restaurant business, as well as other businesses comprising of cosmetic and fashion business. The company was formerly known as As-me ESTELLE CO., LTD. Estelle Holdings Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Ohki Healthcare Holdings Co.,Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of pharmaceuticals and other products. The company is involved in the sale, import, and export of pharmaceuticals, quasi-drugs, medical equipment, cosmetics, poisons, deleterious substances, chemicals, reagents, measuring instruments, optical equipment, electrical appliances, and communication equipment; and veterinary drugs, veterinary quasi-drugs, veterinary medical equipment, pet supplies, feed, and feed additives. It also sells, imports, and exports textile products, hygiene products, cosmetics, beauty products, health equipment, sporting goods, leisure goods, nursing care products, convenient nursing care products, toys, stationery, household goods, daily necessities, food products, food additives, alcoholic beverages, rice, salt, and tobacco; and sale of pesticides, fertilizers, gardening supplies, and agricultural supplies. In addition, the company engages in warehouse operations; development, sale, and rental of information processing systems, logistics system equipment, and software; management and dispensing of pharmacies and drugstores; provision of nursing care services; manufacturing of sanitary products and soft drinks; and manufacture and wholesale of products, such as the polystyrene foam volume reduction liquid, Ecokaton 50-IPS and the antibacterial and disinfectant, Power Anti. Further, it is involved in the wholesale of food and contact lenses, as well as cosmetics and miscellaneous goods; retail of pharmaceuticals; and application for licenses and permits. Ohki Healthcare Holdings Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1658 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sansei Landic Co.,Ltd engages in the real estate business in Japan. The company is involved in the planning, purchasing, sale, and management of the limited property rights of the land. It also procures and sells real estate properties that involve rights adjustments relating to leasehold land, properties with existing residence, and other pieces of real estate. The company was formerly known as Sansei Service Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Sansei Landic Co.,Ltd in February 1997. Sansei Landic Co.,Ltd was incorporated in 1976 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Komori Corporation engages in the manufacture, sale, and repair of printing presses in Japan, North America, Europe, and Greater China. The company offers include offset printing presses, including the LITHRONE, ENTHRONE, SPICA, and system series; digital printing systems that include Impremia series; KP-Connect series; H-UV series; CMS based on offset printing systems; print quality control systems comprising KHS-AI, PDC, PQA, PCC, and KID series; currency printing presses; CHAMBON series packaging printing presses; and PEPIO series gravure offset printing machines. In addition, it provides post press machines, such as folding machines, cutter and cutting systems, pile turner, and blanking systems. Komori Corporation was founded in 1923 and is headquartered in Sumida, Japan.

OM2 Network Co.,Ltd. retails meat and prepared food products in Japan. The company is involved in the meat processing activities; meat product retail; food products retail; as well as offers private label products, such as sauce, hotpot soup, and other products. It operates meat stores; various restaurants; and Japan operations management and operation guidance and store development. OM2 Network Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1958 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Shinsho Corporation imports, exports, and trades in iron and steel, ferrous raw material, nonferrous metal, machinery, information industry, and welding products worldwide. The company provides iron and steel products, including half-finished steel, special steel, construction material, casting and forging, carbon steel, wire rod, titanium and nickel alloy, and casting material and cast products; ferrous raw materials, such as materials for making iron and steel, and other products comprising titanium sponge, coke breeze, slag products, slag powder for blast furnaces, cement, refuse paper and plastic fuel, secondary cement products, iron sand, plastics, chemical products, titanium scrap, new materials, and other auxiliary materials; and nonferrous metals, which includes aluminum and cast products, aluminum and copper material, copper, and processed aluminum and copper products. It also offers machinery and electronics that include chemicals and food processing equipment; iron and steel, and non-ferrous metal related equipment; electronic and IT equipment; environment and energy-related equipment; and general industrial equipment. In addition, the company provides welding products, such as welding materials and welding-related equipment, and production materials. Shinsho Corporation was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Tokyo Automatic Machinery Works, Ltd. designs, develops, produces, and sells various types of packaging machines in Japan and internationally. It also offers tobacco production and packaging machinery; compression packaging machines; assembly machines; charter flight packing machine; filling machine; line system; caser; palletizer; letter making machine; cartoner; paper product packaging machine; tobacco machine; baler; vibrating conveyor; and production machinery. The company was formerly known as Shimane Manufacturing Co. and changed its name to Tokyo Automatic Machinery Works, Ltd. in 1949. The company was founded in 1908 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon BS Broadcasting Corporation engages in the BS digital broadcasting business in Japan. The company sells the broadcast time slots of programs to advertisers, and broadcast programs provided by advertisers and their commercials; time slots between programs to advertisers in seconds, and broadcast advertiser's commercials; and programs to local terrestrial stations. Its program categories include news, educational, entertainment, advertisements, shopping, and other programs. The company also engages in the program production; Internet distribution business of programs and related content; and publishing business of children's books, including picture books, reading materials, and educational materials. The company was formerly known as Nippon BS Broadcasting Kikaku Corporation and changed its name to Nippon BS Broadcasting Corporation in February 2007. The company was incorporated in 1999 and is based in Tokyo, Japan. Nippon BS Broadcasting Corporation is a subsidiary of Bic Camera Co., Ltd.

GEOMATEC Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells thin film products in Japan. The company offers anti-reflection and anti-glare, including g.moth, cover panels, and anti-reflective (AR) coatings; optical reflection, transmission, separation and synthesis, such as two-way mirrors, electrode films for electrochromic mirrors, metallic mirrors and high reflectivity coatings, UV cut-off filters and cold mirrors, beam splitters, and dichroic filters (mirrors) and band-pass filters; light shielding products; and laser mirrors, laser beam expanders, and f-theta lenses. It also provides inorganic high-resistance transparent conductive films, and electromagnetic wave shield coatings; transparent conducting electrodes; metal electrodes; and fine-circuit formation and patterning. In addition, the company provides transparent heaters, and metallic flexible thin-film heaters; thin-film flow sensors, and capacitive proximity sensors; water and oil repellents; chemical-resistant, corrosion-resistant and high-hardness; metallization; and decorative films. It operates through online store. The company serves devices, displays and touch screens; semiconductors, electronics, and IOT; railway, aviation, and marine; automotive; public spaces, and transportation infrastructure; stores, offices, and houses; museums and exhibitions; decorations and watches; and medical and healthcare industries. GEOMATEC Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1953 and is headquartered in Yokohama, Japan.

Papyless Co., Ltd. sells and rents e-books in Japan. It operates through E-Book and IP production segments. The company provides comics, vertical comics, picture novels, comic theaters, novels/practical books, magazines, and gravures. It also develops and produces next-generation content; and operates e-book agency. The company was incorporated in 1995 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Asmo Corporation processed, imports and sells meat products in Japan, Taiwan, and China. It operates through ASMO Business; ASMO Trading Business; ASMO Food Services Business; ASMO service business; ASMO nursing care service business; and ASMOCATERING (HK) business. It provides meals in various fields, such as hospital meals, office meals, school meals, and welfare facility meals for the elderly. The company also offers nursing care services; manages 10 Japanese restaurants in Hong Kong; and provides insurance products. Additionally, it engages in trust business, such as management of property for the elderly and maintenance of lump-sum payment for paid elderly home, as well as real estate leasing business. Further, the company operates home-visit and care establishments, pay nursing homes, and restaurants. The company was incorporated in 1975 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Asmo Corporation operates as a subsidiary of PersonsBridge Co., Ltd.

Kaneko Seeds Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, production and sale agricultural seeds in Japan. It operates through Seeds and Seedlings Business, Flower Business, Agricultural Materials Business, and Facility Materials Business segments. The company offers bulbs, and flower and horticultural supplies; vegetable and pasture seeds, virus-free seedlings, and seed potatoes; grass, forage crop, green manure crop, landscaping, and turfgrass seeds; and greenhouses and agricultural production materials. It also sells flower seedlings and vegetable seeds; young tomatoes and carnations; pesticides and coated fertilizers; and agricultural chemicals, resin materials, and hydroponic cultivation systems. In addition, the company engages in the sale and repair of greenhouse horticulture equipment; design, construct, and sale of hydroponic cultivation plants; and landscaping and slope construction contracting work. It serves home gardening enthusiasts and commercial growers; and home centers, gardening specialty stores, and gardening wholesalers. Kaneko Seeds Co., Ltd. was founded in 1895 and is headquartered in Maebashi, Japan.

Ohmura Shigyo Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells paper container packaging materials in Japan. It offers sheets, cases, cardboard pallets, and label products, as well as cat tunnel house products; and cardboard plates and dies. The company was founded in 1965 and is headquartered in Chigasaki, Japan.

Kyoei Steel Ltd. engages in the manufacture, processing, and sells of billets and steel products in Japan, Vietnam, North America, and internationally. It operates through three segments: Domestic Steel Business, Overseas Steel Business, and Material Recycling Business. The company offers concrete reinforcing bars; merchant bars, such as flat bars, I beams, equal angle bars, and round bars; billets; and threaded rebars and joints for threaded rebars. It also involved in the collection, transportation, and treatment of general, industrial, and medical waste; and manufactures and sells casting products. In addition, the company engages in the manufacturing and sale of recycled crushed ballast, construction materials, and steel products and raw/secondary materials; contracting business for in-plant loading and unloading; automobile transportation and insurance agency businesses; collection, processing, and sales of iron, aluminum, and nonferrous metal scraps; and construction and operation of multi-purposed seaport. Kyoei Steel Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Nihon Enterprise Co.,Ltd. plans, develops, and operates content for smartphone and other multi devices. It offers ATIS Traffic Info, a drive recorder function that records for traffic condition information; Women's Diary, a health care app for women; and Inaseri Ichiba website for general consumers that sells fisheries products, fruits, and vegetables. The company also provides business support services, such as kitting support, traffic information, and sound solution services; and application and system planning, development, and management services for enterprise customers to streamline their business operations, as well as advertising agent services. In addition, the company engages in the renewable power generation business. Nihon Enterprise Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1989 and is headquartered in Shibuya, Japan.

PRAP Japan, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a public relations consultancy company in Japan. It provides communication; digital solution; and media training and crisis management services. PRAP Japan, Inc. was incorporated in 1970 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Sakai Heavy Industries, Ltd. manufactures and sells of construction equipment and industrial machinery in Japan and internationally. The company offers soil compactors, such as vibratory single drum rollers; asphalt rollers, including static three wheel rollers, static pneumatic tire rollers, vibratory tandem rollers, and combined rollers; road maintenance equipment, comprising road cutter, road planner, and soil stabilizers, water sprinkler, and cold recyclers; and light equipment, which include walk behind rollers, plate compactors, and rammers. It is also involved in the sale of outsourced industrial machinery. The company was founded in 1918 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Sanyo Industries, Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of metallic building materials in Japan. The company  is involved in the manufacture, sale, and contract work of metallic building materials, and internal and external materials; design and manufacture of air-conditioning and ventilation systems, and associated equipment; sale and contract work of solar energy equipment; and export and import of other related products and materials. It also offers aluminum products, including handrails, copings, louvers, and spandrail; system floors, such as multipurpose, magic, deck, and condominium floors; and natural ventilation products; and metal flame, natural lighting, system ceiling, filler, and wire and metal products. Sanyo Industries, Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

JICHODO Co.,Ltd. engages in planning, manufacturing, and sale of uniforms in Japan and internationally. It also offers men's casuals clothes, medical white coats, and safety shoes. The company was founded in 1924 and is based in Fukuyama, Japan.

Kyokuto Boeki Kaisha, Ltd. primarily operates as an engineering trading company in Japan and internationally. It is involved in the import, export, wholesale, sale, and agent services related to machinery, equipment, materials, measuring instruments, gauges, vessels, vehicles, hardware, chemical engineering products, explosives, foodstuffs, textiles and textile products, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and other general merchandise; aircrafts; space appliances and parts; computers and parts; power generation facilities, control devices, and monitoring devices and parts; bridges; boilers; turbines; communication systems; pollution control devices, such as water treatment devices and atmospheric contamination analyzers; devices related to natural resource exploration, analysis and excavation, and parts; alcoholic beverages and drinking water; agrochemicals, herbicides, feeds, feed additives, fertilizers, and fertilizer additives and related materials; and fuel, medical supplies, fuel cells, and related materials. The company also provides repair and maintenance services related to the machinery, devices, and facilities; engages in acquisition, planning, licensing, sale, repair, maintenance, and brokering of computer system technology and software; and computer-based information processing and advertising activities. In addition, it offers general leasing, transportation, construction contracting, temporary staffing, and life and non-life insurance services. Kyokuto Boeki Kaisha, Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Kunimine Industries Co., Ltd. engages in the mining, manufacture, and sale of bentonite ore in Japan. It operates through Bentonite, Clay Science, and Agriculture segments. The company is involved in the extraction, processing, and sale of rocks and clay; and manufacturing, processing, and sale of inorganic and organic industrial chemicals, agricultural chemicals, medicines, quasi-drugs, and drugs intended for animal use. It also engages in the manufacture, processing, and sale of food, food additives, feed, and feed additives; and design, construction, and operation of plants related to powder and granular material equipment, molding devices and civil engineering equipment, etc.; and manufacture and sale of related equipment and materials. In addition, the company manufactures and sells water treatment and waste water/sewage treatment agents, as well as operates and constructs treatment equipment. Further, it manufactures and sells bentonite-based products for castings, civil engineering and construction, toilet sand for pets, cosmetics, life sciences, and waste disposal. Additionally, the company manufactures, processes, and sells agrochemicals and agrochemical carriers. Kunimine Industries Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1943 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Seikagaku Corporation manufactures and sells pharmaceuticals and medical devices primarily related to glycoconjugates in Japan and internationally. It operates in two segments, Pharmaceuticals and LAL Business. The company offers pharmaceuticals and medical devices, including intra-articular injections for improving joint functions under the brands of ARTZ, ARTZ Dispo, VISCO-3, JOYCLU Gel-One, Hylink, and SUPARTZ FX; ophthalmic surgical aids under the OPEGAN and SHELLGAN brands; surgical aids for endoscopic mucosal resection under the brand name of MucoUp; and HERNICORE for the treatment of lumbar disc herniation. It also offers bulk products comprising sodium hyaluronate and sodium chondroitin sulfate for use in pharmaceuticals and cosmetics. In addition, the company develops endotoxin-detecting reagents for use in quality control of injectable pharmaceuticals and medical devices manufacturing processes and water quality control of dialysate used in artificial dialysis. Further, its products under development include SI-6603 for lumbar disc herniation; SI-614, a dry eye treatment; SI-613 for osteoarthritis; SI-722 to treat interstitial cystitis and bladder pain syndrome; SI-613-ETP to treat enthesopathy; and SI-449, a cross-linked chondroitin sulfate adhesion barrier. The company was formerly known as K.K. Seikagaku Kenkyusho and changed its name to Seikagaku Corporation in 1962. Seikagaku Corporation was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Meiji Electric Industries Co.,Ltd. imports, exports, and sells electrical devices, measuring instruments, electrical equipment, and automation and energy-saving function components and equipment. It also designs, develops, and manufactures electronic products, inspection, mechatronic, FA, and information and distribution systems. In addition, the company offers measurement, control, and information processing consulting services; and maintenance services for industrial instrumentation and electrical measurement devices. The company was incorporated in 1920 and is headquartered in Nagoya, Japan.

Almedio Inc. engages in the research, development, manufacture, and sale of test tapes and test discs in Japan. It operates through Material, Archive, and Nano Material business segments. The Insulating Material segment manufactures and sells refractory materials, thermal insulators, and related products. The Storage Solution segment develops, manufactures, and sells optical drives mounted. The Nano Material segment develops, produces, and processes nanomaterial products, such as carbon nano fiber/ carbon microfiber, synthetic graphene powder, CNF pellets for aviation / space, automobile, medical products, and sports / leisure goods industries. The company research, develops, manufactures, and sells measuring instruments for optical media and industrial equipment for the optical disc drives. In addition, it provides attestation tests for the DVD verification laboratory; and tests for various kinds of discs. The company was formerly known as A-BEX and changed its name to Almedio Inc. in March 1997. Almedio Inc. was incorporated in 1981 and is based in Kunitachi, Japan.

Maezawa Kyuso Industries Co.,Ltd. designs, manufactures, and sells water supply equipment in Japan. It operates through the Water Supply Equipment, Housing & Building Equipment, and Product Sales segments. The company produces and sells water supply equipment for city waterworks, such as products for drawing water from distribution pipes to residential and commercial premises. It also provides hot water piping components for indoor use, floor heating components, and related products, as well as plumbing equipment materials for homes and buildings. The company was founded in 1937 and is headquartered in Meguro, Japan.

T.Kawabe & Co., Ltd. manufactures, sells, imports, and exports handkerchiefs, scarves, mufflers, towels, and fabric products in Japan. It operates in two segments, Personal Goods Business and Fragrance Business. The company also engages in the acquisition, rental, and sale of intellectual property rights, such as patents, utility models, designs, and trademarks, as well as manufactures, sells, and imports and exports perfumes. The company was founded in 1923 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. T.Kawabe & Co., Ltd. is a subsidiary of Ichihiro Co., Ltd.

Sec Carbon,Limited engages in the manufacture and sale of graphite electrodes for steel making. It offers SK-B graphitized cathode blocks for aluminum smelting; graphite electrodes that are used in electric arc furnaces for steelmaking; and carbon and graphite specialties and materials for use in the chemical, electrochemistry, machine, and metallurgy industries. The company also provides fine powder that is used in various industries, such as automobile paint, sliding materials, metallurgic uses, and batteries. In addition, it is also involved in research and development related to molten salt electrolysis. The company was formerly known as SEC Corporation and changed its name to Sec Carbon,Limited in October 2006. Sec Carbon,Limited was incorporated in 1934 and is headquartered in Amagasaki, Japan.

FJ NEXT Holdings Co., Ltd. engages in the planning, development, sale, and brokerage of real estate properties in Japan. It operates in four segments: Real Estate Development, Real Estate Management, Construction, and Japanese Inn. The company is also involved in the development of condominiums and resorts; real estate management activities; design, construction, and renovation of building and detached houses; construction management and consulting business; and operation of inns under the Ito Yuki Tei, Ito Yuki Tei Kawana Bettei, Gyokuhokan, and Seiryuso names. In addition, it engages in non-life insurance agency business; restaurant business; cottage-area management operations; and provision of financial services, such as loans. The company was formerly known as FJ Next Co., Ltd. and changed its name to FJ NEXT Holdings Co., Ltd. in October 2021. FJ NEXT Holdings Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1980 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Mars Group Holdings Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the manufacture, sale, and provision of services for peripheral devices for amusement-related facilities in Japan. It operates through the Amusement Related Business, Smart Solution Related Business, and Hotel and Restaurant Related Business segments. The company plans, develops, manufactures, and sells automatic recognition systems, such as RFID, bar code, two-dimensional code, and X-ray inspection systems; designs, manufactures, and sells electronic and information equipment, banknote validators, card readers, vending machines, and card printers; plans and sells information processing equipment; content distribution business; and provides information processing services. It is also involved in the creation, operation, sale, and management of membership management, and provision of related information services; press processing; manufacture and sale of molds and machinery; third-party management of card systems; software development business; design and contracting, construction, and supervision of construction works; management of hotels and restaurants; real estate leasing and management; and planning, production, and sale of printed materials for advertising. In addition, the company engages in the sales and after-sales services of amusement-related equipment. The company was incorporated in 1974 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

ReYuu Japan Inc. engages in trading and distribution of reused communication terminal devices in Japan. The company retails smartphones under iPhone, Galaxy, and Xperia brands; and tablets and PCs under iPad and MacBook brands, as well as new and unused outlet goods. It also provides integrated subscription, kitting, and corporate purchasing services. In addition, the company engages in the restoration of phones; operation of an online shop and mobile refurbishment center; and rental of communication terminals and equipment for corporations. It sells its products to MVNO operators, mobile phone agencies, wholesalers, retailers, companies, and individuals through its own website and external EC malls. ReYuu Japan Inc. was formerly known as Nippon Telephone Inc. and changed its name to ReYuu Japan Inc. in February 2024. The company was incorporated in 1988 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Seven Industries Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells housing materials using laminated timber primarily in Japan. The company offers wooden interior building materials, such as stairs, counters, handrails, Japanese-style room fittings, entrance construction materials, and various fixtures. It also manufactures and sells wooden building structures. The company was incorporated in 1961 and is headquartered in Minokamo, Japan.

Japan Insulation Co., Ltd. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of calcium silicate products in Japan. It also manufactures and sells non-combustible building materials; and designs and constructs steel frame fireproof coating work, as well as heat and cold insulation, and fireproof construction products. The company also offers fireproof coating, interior building, and multifunctional materials. Japan Insulation Co., Ltd. was founded in 1914 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

W-SCOPE Corporation manufactures and sells plastic films and sheets in Japan. The company offers separators for lithium-ion secondary batteries, that is polyolefin microporous membranes. It also markets its products in Korea, the United States, and Europe. The company was incorporated in 2005 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Yushin Company engages in the development, production, sale, and maintenance of Cartesian robots and other industrial equipment and systems for factory automation in Japan and internationally. It offers take out robots, including multi-function, swing type take-out, side entry, horizontal molding, vertical molding machine, and sprue picker robots; palletizing robots for injection-molded products and peripheral equipment; end-of-arm tools; stock systems comprising stockers and conveyors; and downstream equipment, such as gate cut system, assembly, and insert workpiece feeder, as well as labor-saving automation equipment. The company also provides custom-ordered equipment; and parts and maintenance services. The company was formerly known as Yushin Precision Equipment Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Yushin Company in April 2025. Yushin Company was founded in 1971 and is headquartered in Kyoto, Japan.

Nihon Flush Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells interior products and construction materials in Japan. The company offers various interior, flush, sliding, folding, open, and L-shaped doors; storage systems comprising entrance, food, western-style, and toilet, as well as bedding storage; and building materials. Nihon Flush Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1964 and is based in Komatsushima, Japan.

Tenox Corporation engages in the construction business in Japan. The company provides foundation works; and civil engineering and construction consulting services. It also sells concrete piles and steel pipe piles; and rents equipment. In addition, it offers contracting services for ground improvement work. Further, the company engages in real estate rental business. The company was incorporated in 1970 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Sasatoku Printing Co.,Ltd. engages in the planning, designing, editing, plate making, and printing of various printed materials in Japan. The company is also involved in bookbinding, paper container processing, image processing, packaging and kitting for shipping. The company was founded in 1890 and is based in Toyoake, Japan.

KEL Corporation engages in the manufacture and sale of industrial connectors in Japan. It offers board to board, board to cable, and on-board power connectors for use in camera/video related, FA equipment, automobile, medical/healthcare, infrastructure/railway, and amusement related applications, as well as provide rack products. The company is also engaged in research and development, production, quality control, and sales activities. Its products have applications in automotive, imaging, medical, and gaming equipment. KEL Corporation was incorporated in 1962 and is based in Tama, Japan.
Home Position Co., Ltd. engages in the detached house sales business. The company was incorporated in 1989 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Mansei Corporation provides electronic products in Japan. The company offers power control equipment, such as AC servos, inverters, etc.; control equipment, including sequencers, etc.; power distribution equipment comprising breakers, transformers, etc.; and rotary equipment which includes motors, industrial fans, etc. It also provides industrial robots, wire cutting machinery, NC discharge processing machinery, laser processing machinery, and CNC equipment. In addition, the company offers design through installation and follow-up servicing of equipment; semiconductors and device products; system and software development; and electronic product contract manufacturing service, as well as Multimedia and information technology. Mansei Corporation was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Kitoku Shinryo Co., Ltd. engages in the procurement, processing, sale, and import/export of rice and rice products, animal feed, hen's eggs in Japan and internationally. The company operates through the Rice Business, Feed Business, Egg Business, and Food Business segments. It provides commercially polished rice, household polished rice, brown rice, minimum-access and processed rice; feed and feed ingredients; eggs for home and commercial use; processed eggs; rice flour, processed foods, protein-adjusted rice, and wheat flour; and corn oil. Kitoku Shinryo Co., Ltd. was founded in 1882 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sanshin Electronics Co., Ltd. sells and trades in electronic components and other devices in Japan and internationally. The company offers integrated circuits, semiconductor elements, and general electronic components; and electronic devices, such as office-automation equipment, communications equipment, and video equipment. It also plans, designs, and manufactures IoT and Edge AI devices; and develops applied software for microcontrollers and simulations for semi-custom LSIs. In addition, the company provides IT infrastructure solutions, including WAN/LAN/mobile, virtualization, cloud, office communication, security, and operational support services; video solutions, such as live relay systems, program transmission systems, IPTV systems, nonlinear editing systems, studio systems, and various video solutions; and various support and services, including call-center services, maintenance-support services, and system-configuration services. Further, the company provides application services in the areas of data analysis systems; call-center systems; individual system development; cloud/software as a service; business systems comprising sales, accounting, payroll, attendance, production, etc.; and groupware consisting of e-mail, schedule management, facility reservation, etc. Additionally, it offers housing, cloud computing, system integration, and operation and maintenance services, as well as support services for construction of networks/circuits. It serves electronic makers, newspaper publishing companies, broadcast stations, government offices, schools, etc. Sanshin Electronics Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1951 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd., through its subsidiaries, engages in the rubber, chemical, green technology, industrial products, and life science businesses in Japan and internationally. The company offers rubbers, including synthetic and silicone rubbers, thermoplastic elastomers and resins, rubber reinforcement and fillers, flame retardants, specialty rubber chemicals, and other rubber materials and machinery. It also provides chemicals, such as waxes and environmentally friendly waxes, silane coupling agents, specialty methacrylates, environmentally friendly TMP and pentaerythritol, photoinitiators, UV absorbers/photostabilizers, antioxidants, flame retardants, urethane/epoxy curing agents, carbodiimide and related products, plasticizers, HOT SBR, NBR latex, resins, pigments, extender pigments, and defoaming and dispersing agents. In addition, the company offers seat heaters, seat ventilation systems, ECUs, lumbar support systems, sensors, suspension mats, seat adjuster motors, latch release actuators, gas springs, automotive benchmarking, and vital sensing sensors; powder and biomass related equipment; and drilling, offshore survey, and offshore wind power equipment. Further, it provides life science related, sustainable, and advanced technology related materials for use in electronics/batteries, air fresheners, pet food, and diapers; genomics/DNA and RNA experimental devices and reagents, proteomics/protein experimental devices and reagents, cell biology/cell experimental devices and reagents, organic synthesis and analytical instruments, single-cell analysis/spatial gene expression analysis, and bioinformatics data analysis. Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Yashima & Co.,Ltd. engages in the sale, maintenance, and import/export of railway related products and electronic parts for industrial machines in Japan and internationally. The company operates through two segments, Railway Business and General Business. It offers tachometer generators, connectors, optical harness, sensors, door switches, terminal boards, power supply devices, polycarbonate window units, compressors for air conditioning, brake parts, wheelsets, wipers, flooring materials, and other electrical and mechanical parts and maintenance equipment. The company sells its products to railway operators, railway vehicle manufacturers, manufacturers of electrical components for railway vehicles and body equipment, industrial equipment manufacturers, electric power equipment manufacturers, automotive manufacturers, and commercial equipment mail order business operators. Yashima & Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1948 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Ryoden Corporation sells factory automation (FA) systems, cooling and heating systems, information and communication technologies (ICT), building systems, smart-agri, green network, healthcare, and electronics businesses in Japan and internationally. The company offers industrial mechatronics/machining equipment, vision sensors, power receiving and distribution equipment, power controllers, drive devices, cloud based integrated monitoring and control, sensors, transformers and static capacitors, edge computing products, industrial robots, displays and SCADA, deep learning products; HAVC and residential equipment, and equipment for low-temperature applications. It also provides beacons, indoor positioning systems, ICT components, and IoT and monitoring solutions; elevators and escalators, security camera systems, and finger authentication devices; and building, parking-facility, and transport equipment. In addition, the company offers semiconductors, communication equipment and modules, optical devices, circuit boards, finished metal products, coils and transformers, connectors and sockets, capacitors, plastics, liquid crystal oscillators, textile products, cast and wrought products, resistors, power sources and batteries, cables and heaters, chemical products, fuses, filters, motors, and other electrical and electronic products. Further, it provides image data management and operational support systems for health care business. It serves construction, automotive, consumer, power and energy, information and communication, transportation, food, and healthcare industries. The company was formerly known as Ryoden Trading Company, Limited and changed its name to Ryoden Corporation in June 2016. Ryoden Corporation was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Cosmos Initia Co., Ltd. engages in the real estate sale, rental, and brokerage activities in Japan. It operates through four segments: Residential Business, Solution Business, Accommodation Business, and Construction Business. The company develops and sells condominium and single-family houses; post completion/move-in maintenance services; renovates existing condominiums; manages rental properties; MIMARU apartment-style hotels; and develops and operates apartment-style hotels. The company was formerly known as Recruit Cosmos Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Cosmos Initia Co., Ltd. in September 2006. Cosmos Initia Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1969 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Pigment Holdings Company Limited manufactures and sells liquid dispersion products, colorants, and resin compounds in Japan and internationally. The company offers various resin compounds, such as olefin and styrene resins, and engineering plastic products; resin colorants for automobile interior and exterior parts, as well as plastic containers, such as shampoo, films, sheets, and fibers; and colorants for automotive interior coverings, sealing materials, flooring materials, etc. It also provides liquid dispersions for FPD colour filters, etc.; and other resin colorants. The company was formerly known as Nippon Pigment Company Limited and changed its name to Nippon Pigment Holdings Company Limited in October 2024. The company was founded in 1925 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Ledax Co.,Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the purchase, sale, and distribution of automobiles in Japan, Asia, Oceania, Africa, and internationally. It operates through Automobile distribution business, and Leaseback-related business segments. The company engages in the purchase and sale of used cars through its retail stores; sale of various property and casualty insurance products comprising car insurance; wholesale and distribution of spare parts and accessories of automobiles through online shop; management of automobile aftermarket parts of online distribution; and export of used cars through internet site, PicknBuy24.com. It also offers management consulting, M&A advisory, outsourcing, and investment services. The company was formerly known as Carchs Holdings Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Ledax Co.,Ltd. in September 2024. Ledax Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1977 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Weds Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the automobile-related wholesale business in Japan. It operates through Automobile Related Sales Business, Logistics Business, Automotive Retail Business, and Welfare segments. The company is involved in the wholesale business, such as after-sales service of automobile parts, automobile wheels, and car accessory parts; manufactures steel wheels; sale of automobile wheel parts; manufacture and sale of high-end aluminum wheels; and logistics business, including storage and loading of wheels and other original products. It also provides warehousing services; wheels and car accessory parts retail business; welfare services for the elderly; and operates as a real estate rental company. The company was formerly known as Nippo Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Weds Co., Ltd. in November 1973. Weds Co., Ltd. was founded in 1965 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Mitachi Co., Ltd. provides and sells semiconductors, electronic components, and electronics manufacturing services in Japan and internationally. The company engages in the sale of semiconductors, LCDs, as well as electronics components and motors for various electronics industries, such as amusement, industrial, consumer, and communication equipment, as well as car electronics and other industries. It also manufactures and sells electronic devices and modules; and assembly equipment for electronic devices, including SMT line and inspection systems. Mitachi Co., Ltd was founded in 1972 and is headquartered in Nagoya, Japan.

Punch Industry Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells mold and die components in Japan and internationally. The company offers components for plastic molds, such as ejector pins and sleeves; center and core pins; date stamps/stoppers for cavity insert; angular pins/locking blocks; slide cores/guide rails; sprue bushings/locating rings; runner, gate, and ejector space components; mold and die opening controllers; cooling and heating items; positioning and leader components; die opening controllers; screws and mold accessories; coil springs; and electrodes and chemical products. It also provides components for press molds, including punch guide bushings; punches and dies for forming; special shaped, precision, and carbide punches and dies; retainers; stripper bolts; related components of misfeed sensors; coil and urethane springs; stripper guide pins and bushings; guide posts and bushings for die sets; components for guide lifters, holder guide post sets, automobile dies, and transfer dies; hooks and components for die storage; and cam units and wear plates. In addition, the company offers special order parts. The company's dies are used in a range of industries such as automobiles, and electrical and electronic equipment; plastic mold products are used in manufacturing plastic products, including exterior parts of plastic models and mobile phones; and press dies that are used to manufacture metal components, which include housing of batteries and car body panels. The company was incorporated in 1975 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

MT Genex Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in renovation of office buildings and real estate management in Japan. It operates through four segments: Renovation, Parking Lots, Facility Maintenance and Management, and Insurance Agency. The company is involved in operation and management of parking lots; proposal and construction of energy-saving LED for office lighting in the renovation work of office buildings and hotels, etc.; and building management, equipment management, cleaning, security, office work, ordering of toilet paper and other hygiene consumables, inspection of fire prevention objects, and operation and management of vending machines. The company was founded in 1945 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. MT Genex Corporation operates as a subsidiary of Mori Trust Co., Ltd.

Ichikawa Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells paper making and industrial felts in Japan and internationally. It offers paper making press felts and belts, and shoe press and transfer belts; industrial products, such as heat-resistant conveyance equipment and cushioning materials for use in the car bodies, internet of things, 5th generation, carports, consumer electronics, smartphones, tablets, aluminum sash products, printed circuit boards, flexible boards, and clothing; and felts for slate. The company was founded in 1918 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

J-Stream Inc. engages in planning, production, licensing, and sale of various digital content and publications in Japan. The company provides content delivery services, including the distribution of video and audio content, such as music, movies, events, corporate briefings, education, and advertisements through the Internet; and advertising focuses on video ads, production, media planning and other services. It is also involved in the content planning, video recording/editing, website production, encoding, and direction on online live sites; voice recording; digitization of existing videos and conversion of video files; and web-based membership information, e-commerce, and settlement services. In addition, the company engages in the planning, development, operation, production, sales, import/export, leasing, and agent business of web-based hardware, software, and other services; planning, production, and agency business related to advertising; and delivers web-based video data and various other forms of information. J-Stream Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Odawara Engineering Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the design, development, manufacture, and sale of motor and coil winding equipment in Japan and internationally. The company operates through Winding Machine Business and Blowers and Housing Equipment Related Business segment. It provides small blowers, waterproof lighting fixtures, and other housing-related equipment. The company also offers hairpin, inserter, flyer, part rotate, needle, and bobbin products. It serves its products for use in home appliances, automobiles, industrial and medical equipment, OA/AV equipment, and communications. Odawara Engineering Co., Ltd. was founded in 1950 and is headquartered in Ashigarakami, Japan.

LEOCLAN Co.,Ltd. operates as a medical equipment trading company that meets various medical care, welfare, and health needs. It operates through three segments: Medical Total Solutions Business, Remote Image Diagnosis Service Business, and School Lunch Business. The Medical Total Solutions Business segment provides incidental maintenance services; and interior decoration construction and installation works for medical institutions, preventive/health checkup facilities, nursing care and welfare facilities, etc. based on consulting services, such as selection of medical equipment, as well as sells medical equipment and medical information systems. The Remote Image Diagnosis Service Business segment offers services in which diagnostic radiology specialists remotely diagnose image data taken by MRI, CT, etc., and provides information. The School Lunch Business segment provides school lunch services, including regular meals, medical meals, and other meals to nursing care and welfare facilities. LEOCLAN Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in Settsu, Japan.

Sonec Corporation engages in the general construction business. It constructs medical, welfare, education/childcare/cultural, commercial, production/logistics, and religious facilities, as well as housing complexes. The company also engages in the civil engineering construction businesses, such as road and bridge, and river construction activities. In addition, it offers building repair and maintenance, and other renewal services. Further, the company is involved in design and construction supervision; and lending of construction machinery and equipment. Sonec Corporation was incorporated in 1944 and is headquartered in Takasago, Japan.

Maezawa Kasei Industries Co., Ltd. produces and sells water and sewerage related products, and environmental equipment. It operates through three segments: Pipes and Engineering Plastic, Water/Environmental Engineering, and Plastic Molding. The company manufactures and sells fitting and a PVC catch basin with an invert; and parts for housing equipment and various parts for plastic products on a build-to-order basis. It also designs, constructs, maintains, and manages combined wastewater treatment plants, industrial wastewater treatment facilities, plumbing and sanitary equipment, and pump plants. The company was founded in 1937 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Japan Cash Machine Co., Ltd. develops, manufactures, and sells of currency handling machines and equipment for amusement facilities in Japan and internationally. It operates through Global Gaming, Overseas Commercials, Domestic Commercials, and recreation hall equipment segments. It offers banknote recycler, banknote recycler with four denomination recycling, coin recycler, coin recycler with double sided insertion slot, modular banknote recycler, bill validator, recycler unit, bill validator with banknote storage box, plasma cluster Ion generating unit systems for spacious areas, cash/ cashless multi payment machine flexcom pay, and access control systems. It serves retail, transportation, casinos/gaming, public racing, environment, clinic, and system products markets. Japan Cash Machine Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1955 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Eiken Industries Co.,Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of filters for internal combustion engines and special filters for industrial use in Japan. It offers oil, air, fuel, air conditioner, and cabin filter. The company also provides kitchen equipment part; bunsen, infrared, ceramic, and blast burner; filters for water filtration and dust collectors; and heat exchanger, garage sauna, and household goods. In addition, it offers various filter elements for internal combustion engines of automobiles, construction machinery, and agricultural machinery, special filters for industrial machinery and other industrial applications. The company was formerly known as Ehken Kogyo Corporation and changed its name to Eiken Industries Co.,Ltd. in July 1972. Eiken Industries Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1967 and is headquartered in Omaezaki, Japan.

Wesco Holdings Inc. engages in general construction consulting business in Japan. It is also involved in copying and binding, aquarium management, sports facility management, real estate, and public interest businesses. Wesco Holdings Inc. was founded in 1970 and is headquartered in Okayama, Japan.

Tokyo Radiator Mfg.Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells heat exchangers and body components in Japan, China, and rest of Asia. It offers cooling modules for trucks, as well as construction and industrial machineries; radiators and inter coolers; oil pan; fuel, vacuum, and SCR tanks; EGR cooler; and oil and fuel coolers. The company was incorporated in 1938 and is headquartered in Fujisawa, Japan.

Yokohama Maruuo Co., Ltd. provides marine products in Japan. The company operates through Seafood wholesale business, Marine products sales, Real Estate Leasing, and Shipping Segments. It engages in the wholesale of fishery and marine products in local wholesale markets; distribution of marine products to mass retailers and the foodservice industry; and rental of distribution centers, as well as fishery forwarding activities. Its products include fresh tuna, fresh fish, delicacies and other seafood, distinctive products, frozen products, dried-salted products, semi-dried fish, processed products, and fish-paste products. It also provides transportation service for marine products. Yokohama Maruuo Co., Ltd. was founded in 1947 and is headquartered in Yokohama, Japan.

SystemEXE,Inc. engages in the system integration business. It provides business system development, scratch development, IT infrastructure construction, cloud integration, AI solutions, database services, data utilization, operations and maintenance, PMO support, system migration, DX (digital transformation) solutions, offshore development, groupware workflow, and IoT services, as well as data analysis infrastructure construction and report-based DX promotion solutions. The company serves real estate, manufacturing, non-life and life insurance, medical care, petroleum, chemical, and other industries. SystemEXE,Inc. was incorporated in 1998 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Aida Engineering, Ltd. manufactures and sells press machines, auto-processing lines, industrial robots, auto-conveyers, and dies in Japan, China, rest of Asia, the Americas, and Europe. It offers general-purpose servo, mid-size and large servo, general-purpose, mid-size and large, precision forming, high speed automatic, and cold forging presses, as well as transfer equipment, coil feeders, and tooling dies. The company also provides troubleshooting/spare parts, retrofit/modernization, routine inspection, and overhaul/machine relocation services. In addition, it offers piling systems, servo motors, servo drive technologies, material feeders, die-forming methods, servo die cushions, and line synchronization control technologies. The company was formerly known as AIDA Ironworks and changed its name to Aida Engineering, Ltd. in 1970. Aida Engineering, Ltd. was founded in 1917 and is headquartered in Sagamihara, Japan.

AXELL Corporation designs, manufactures, and sells semiconductor ICs and printed circuit boards with embedded semiconductor ICs in Japan. It is also involved in the research, development, and sale of elemental technologies related to graphics, sound, and machine learning; information securities; and block chain. In addition, the company offers various informational services using the internet; develops and sells software; and designs, manufactures, and sells applied equipment, as well as engages in the secondhand articles trading and other businesses. AXELL Corporation was incorporated in 1996 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

HIRANO TECSEED Co.,Ltd. engages in the production and sale of coating/laminating and industrial machinery in Japan and internationally. The company offers optical function film, flexible board, dry film, electronic material, battery electrode, adhesive, release film and paper, carbon fiber prepreg, synthetic leather, hot-melt, and other coating lines. It also provides non-woven dryers, ceramic sheet forming lines, film stretching lines, glass cloth production lines, and multicoaters. The company was founded in 1935 and is headquartered in Nara, Japan.

Ono Sokki Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells measuring instruments in Japan and internationally. It operates through measuring instruments and custom-made test equipment segments. The company provides laboratory automation, automotive test system, and automotive measurement and analysis tools, such as sensors / measurement equipment, GPS speedometer, combustion analysis system, and sound and vibration analysis system; sound and vibration, torque measurement, flow rate measurement, rpm & speed measurement, and dimensional measurement devices; and data processing & signal analysis products. It also offers custom-made testing equipment for research and development and quality control purposes; consulting services; cloud services related to sound and vibration; after-sales services; and engineering services. In addition, the company engages in non-life insurance agency business; management of land, buildings and equipment, as well as provides other contracted services.
