Chubu Steel Plate Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1950 and is headquartered in Nagoya, Japan.

Shibaura Machine Co.,Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of various machines in Japan and internationally. It operates through three segments: Molding Machinery, Machine Tools, and Control Systems. The company offers injection molding machines, die-casting machines, extrusion machines, nano processing systems, machine tools, Board Type PLC, servo motors, industrial robots, and IoT+m products, as well as provides additive manufacturing systems and engineering solutions. It also provides high precision machine tools, micro pattern imprinting machines, high precision glass mold press machines, and electronic control system. In addition, the company provides machine tools that include double column type machining centers, horizontal boring machines, vertical lathes, and other machine tools. Furthur, it is involved in the castings, heat treatment, and machining work activities. The company was formerly known as Toshiba Machine Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Shibaura Machine Co.,Ltd. in April 2020. Shibaura Machine Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1938 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

YKT Corporation, a machinery trading company, imports and exports machine tools, industrial machinery, measuring instruments, and electronic equipment in Japan and internationally. The company provides products for tool, parts, die and mold, medical parts, and machine machinery, as well as lifestyle/infrastructure, surface treatment, measurement, and after-sales services. It also offers dimensional and geometry measurement, inclination measurement, and inspection equipment; electronic component mounting, semiconductor packaging, and digital assembly; and peripheral devices for tool grinders, turning machines, and machining centers. YKT Corporation was founded in 1924 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Hirose Tusyo Inc. operates as a foreign exchange margin trading company in Japan. IThe company offers option trading services; and white label services for financial instruments business operators, as well as cover trading and over-the-counter securities CFD trading as a counterparty for financial instruments business operators. Hirose Tusyo Inc. was incorporated in 2004 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Koyosha Inc. engages in the printing business in Japan. The company offers printing services, including wide color gamut printing, functional antibacterial varnish printing, digital paper proofreader, and original promotional fan; environmentally friendly printing; large format printing, such as solvent inkjet printer; digital services comprising e-book creation, non-contact scanning, image correction, and photo retouching; and creative services. It is also involved in planning, design, DTP, plate making, digital content production, display, on-demand POP, novelty production, and WcLock businesses. The company was incorporated in 1949 and is headquartered in Bunkyo, Japan.

Kissei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. researches, develops, manufactures, and sells pharmaceutical products primarily in Japan. The company offers Calogra tablets, for the treatment of ulcerative colitis; Tabneos capsules for the treatment of microscopic polyangiitis and granulomatous polyangiitis; Minirin Melt OD tablets; Corsuba Intravenous Dialysis Syringe for the treatment of chronic idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura; Beova tablets for the treatment of overactive bladder treatment; and Darbepoetin Alfa BS injection JCR for the treatment of renal anemia. Its products in pipeline include CG0070, which is in Phase III clinical trial to treat non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer; Linzagolix, which is in Phase III clinical trial for the treatment of uterine fibroids and endometriosis; KDT-3594, which is in Phase II clinical trial to treat Parkinson's disease; KSP-0243 that is in Phase II clinical trial to treat Ulcerative colitis; and Difelikefalin, which is in Phase III clinical trial to treat uremic pruritus in dialysis patients. The company is also developing Silodosin for dysuria associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia; and Fostamatinib for tyrosine kinase inhibitor. In addition, it develops and sells protein controlled and energy supply food; and purchases and sells related materials, as well as offers system development and information processing, construction contracting, facility and equipment management, and information gathering and development support services. Further, the company engages in the information equipment rentals; insurance agency business; manufactures and sells noodles; and undertakes construction of factories, research institutes, offices, etc., as well as computer system design and development, and cloud services. Kissei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. was formerly known as Kissei Yakuhin Kogyo Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Kissei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. in October 1964. The company was incorporated in 1946 and is based in Matsumoto, Japan.

AOI Electronics Co., Ltd. produces and sells electronic parts in Japan, rest of Asia, the United States, and internationally. It offers integrated circuits, optical sensors, wafer-level packages, LEDs, thickfilm thermal printheads, sensors, MEMS devices, and semiconductor packages. The company was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Takamatsu, Japan.

TS TECH Co., Ltd. engages in development, manufacture, and sale of seats for automobiles. The company offers interior trims and components for automobiles; motorcycle seats; and motorcycle parts and accessories. It also provides front seats, mid seats, rear seats, and door trims for automobiles; and leisure vehicle seats and medical chairs. The company operates in Japan, America, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, China, Hong Kong, Thailand, the Philippines, India, Indonesia, Hungary, and Poland. The company was formerly known as Tokyo Seat Co., Ltd. and changed its name to TS TECH Co., Ltd. in October 1997. TS TECH Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1948 and is headquartered in Asaka, Japan.

Ichiken Co., Ltd. engages in the construction business in Japan. The company is involved in businesses related to architecture, civil engineering, paving, interior finishing work, and other general construction works. It also operates real estate business including real estate sales, leasing and other real estate business activities; and engages in planning, designing, and management of complex commercial facilities and specialty stores. Ichiken Co., Ltd. was formerly known as Daiichi Kensetsu Corporation Ltd. and changed its name to Ichiken Co., Ltd. in 1989. The company was incorporated in 1930 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Honyaku Center Inc. provides translation services in Japan and internationally. The company offers translation for patent documents comprising application-related, court-related, and others; and medical documents, including basic research/non-clinical development, clinical development, regulatory submission, and post-marketing. It also provides financial and legal translations comprising finance, legal, disclosure and related documents, and corporate and other documents; and translation services for IT related documents and manuals documents. In addition, the company offers translation of industrial documents, such as R&D, planning, and design documents; production, quality assurance, implementation, and maintenance service documents; sales and marketing documents; and non-manufacturing industry related documents. Further, it provides medical writing, grammar check, transcription, staffing, multilingual contact center, proofreading and back translation, and foreign patent application services, as well as services related to computer games, e-learning, and Websites; and prepares drawings. Additionally, the company offers translation services for VISA application documents required to apply for marriage, work, residency, study abroad, and other visas; and DTP, printing, document, and e-book translation related services, as well as pamphlets and catalogues, press releases and newsletters, and presentation documents. The company serves clients in a range of industries, such as auto, mechanical, electronics, pharma, patent, law, finance, R&D, education, and other fields. Honyaku Center Inc. was incorporated in 1986 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

UEKI Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, provides construction services in Japan. The company operates through three segments: Construction Business, Real Estate Business, and Building Materials Manufacturing and Sales Business. It offers architecture and civil engineering services; contracting, planning, design, construction, maintenance, and management of railway tracks, power generation facilities, and drainage sanitary facilities, as well as plumbing, electrical work, water supply; electrical construction design and contracting; production and maintenance of water treatment equipment; and contracting for machinery and equipment installation and waterworks construction. The company also engages in leasing of temporary construction materials; collection, transportation, and disposal of industrial waste; buying, selling, exchanging, leasing, and brokering land and buildings; home care service and home care support businesses; and golf course management. In addition, it is involved in retail sale of electrical appliances; sale of water treatment agents; software development and sale; information processing and provision services; computer-related consulting; non-life insurance agency; and life insurance solicitation. The company was founded in 1885 and is headquartered in Kashiwazaki, Japan.

Daytona Corporation engages in market research, planning, development, testing, procurement, and logistics management of motorcycle parts and accessories. The company offers brake products comprising brackets, calipers, and rear master cylinders; chassis parts, which include fork bushes, steering dampers, and wheel rims; electrical parts, such as switches, charging systems, and regulators; and engine parts, including cam chain and tensioners, connecting rods, cylinders, intake manifold, lifter blocks, oil pumps, rocker arms, rocker cover kits, switches, and tappet lifters. It also provides gauges comprising oil temp dipstick; hand controls, such as clutch levers, control kits, master cylinders, switch housings, and switches; and master cylinder and caliper rebuild kits. In addition, the company offers options 4valve and 2valve; dress-up; carburetor body and manifold and setting parts; tools and maintenance products; parts and other engine products. In addition, the company engages in the sale of electricity generated from solar power facilities and the sale of renewable energy, as well as reuse sales of motorcycle parts and accessories. The company was formerly known as Henry Abe Industry Co. Ltd. and changed its name to Daytona Co., Ltd. in July 1985. Daytona Corporation was incorporated in 1972 and is based in Shizuoka, Japan.

New Japan Chemical Co., Ltd. produces and sells chemical products in Japan and internationally. It provides oleo-chemicals, including hydrogenated oils, fatty acids and esters, glycerin, alcohols, non-ionic and anion surfactants, and amphoteric surfactants; and plasticizer, such as phthalate and non-phthalate plasticizers, and specialty plasticizers. The company also offers plastic and coating materials, which include clarifiers and nucleating agents, epoxy resin curing agent, epoxy resins, raw materials for resins, methacrylate, and antistatic agents; and lubricants, such as evaporation-resistant and low-viscosity lubricants, dibasic acid esters, and aromatic lubricants. In addition, it is involved in commissioned hydrogenation business. The company was incorporated in 1919 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

CONVUM Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of pneumatic equipment in Japan and internationally. It offers vacuum ejectors; vacuum suction pads; pressure sensors; vacuum robot hand, including balloon hand, e-hand, palletizer, kraft paper bag suction hand, bag suction hand, large diameter pad hand, and robot hand kit; vacuum accessory, such as vacuum filter, work drop prevention check valve, miniature vacuum in-line filter, check valve, vacuum in-line filter with coupling, and ejector silencer; and other products comprising thin non-contact pad, vacuum switching valve, picking pad, and mini air cylinders. The company also provides vacuum pumps; and speed controllers, tubes, push-in connectors, and miniature air cylinders. The company was formerly known as Myotoku Ltd. and changed its name to CONVUM Ltd. in January 2022. CONVUM Ltd. was incorporated in 1951 and is headquartered in Ota, Japan.

Satoh & Co., Ltd. sells commercial food products in Japan. It operates through two segments, Wholesale Division and Retail Division. The company offers confectionery and bakery products; school and industrial lunch, food services supplies for hotels and restaurants; lunch and catering supplies; and logistics services, as well as sells prepared food ingredients for convenience stores, mass retailers, processed agricultural, and livestock products, and processed raw materials. The company was founded in 1945 and is headquartered in Sendai, Japan.

Coach A Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides dialogic organizational development services through systemic coaching in Japan and internationally. The company offers executive coaching (EC), driving corporate dynamism (DCD), three-minute coaching, and dialogue activation for innovation business execution (DAIBE) workshops; and Start Here, your Organizational Transformation (SHOT), a one-on-one coaching service to transform across the organization over a short period. It also provides Core Essentials Program, a foundational to intermediate professional coach training; COACHING CLINIC LICENSING PROGRAM, a four-day live train-the-trainer (T3) licensing program intended for individuals and organizations; and Coaching Session Evaluation Scale, a web-based system used by coaches to evaluate coaching programs. In addition, the company offers Hello, Coaching!, a coaching portal that offers coaching management tips and articles on coaching; COACH A E-Newsletter, a platform that provides information related to coaching, leadership, and transformation; and coaching for health executives/managers. Further, it provides coaching manager development services; and research and studies on leadership and organizational development. Coach A Co., Ltd. was founded in 1997 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

The Zenitaka Corporation provides general contracting services in Japan and internationally. It is involved in the planning, design, and Execution of construction activities; urban, suburban, and marine development operations; and real estate and engineering activities. The Zenitaka Corporation was founded in 1705 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

OUG Holdings Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the wholesale of marine products in Japan. The company distributes marine products primarily to wholesalers, mass retailers, supermarkets, restaurants, hotels, and stores. It is also involved in the aquaculture of yellowtail, sea bream, and amberjack; processing farmed fish fillets and local seafood; sale of live fish; sale of processed products, such as fresh fish, rice, and vegetables; logistics and distribution business; purchase and sale of fresh and frozen shrimp; sale of salt-dried sockeye salmon, mentaiko, cod roe, salmon roe, other fish roe, seafood bowl, and sushi products; and online sale of shrimps, crabs, and eel products. In addition, the company manufactures and sells food products for hotels and restaurants, as well as offers cooking agency services. Further, it is involved in the insurance agency and leasing businesses. The company was formerly known as Osaka Uoichiba Co., Ltd. and changed its name to OUG Holdings Inc. in October 2006. OUG Holdings Inc. was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Takasago Tekko K.K. manufactures and sells various steel products developed primarily from stainless steel in Japan. The company offers cold rolled steel products, including cold rolled steel and cold rolled special steel strips; and electromagnetic plates and sheets, such as soft magnetic iron plates and 1% silicon iron sheets. It also provides embossed stainless steel products comprising RUNNER stainless, ordinary embossed stainless steel, and coated stainless steel products, as well as SUBERANNAR products; and processed stainless steel products that include stainless steel hard plates, high precision pipes, and molded stainless-steel products. It is also involved in real estate rental and management business. The company was incorporated in 1923 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nihon Denkei Co.,Ltd. trades, repairs, and calibrates electronic measuring instruments in Japan, Thailand, Singapore, Vietnam, Malaysia, South Korea, Taiwan, Indonesia, the Philippines, India, China, the United States America, and Germany. The company manufactures, distributes, leases, and rents electronic measuring instruments, system equipment, electronic parts, etc. It also provides science instruments; environment, evaluation, and testing devices; and information equipment. In addition, it offers manufacturing, processing, and inspection equipment. Further, it provides optical, imaging, and precision devices; communication measuring instruments; computers and computer supplies; automate and labor-saving machines; physics and chemistry measuring equipment; and industrial application instruments. The company was incorporated in 1950 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sakae Electronics Corporation trades in general industrial electronic components in Japan. The company offers switching power supply, connector, semiconductor sockets, printed wiring boards, optical products, sensor, fans and resistors, wirewound resistance, terminal blocks and relay parts, digital signage, fuse, accessories, FA and control equipment, motors, PCs and network equipment. It also provides inventer, led lightening power supply, ac adaptor, trance, heat sink, heat exchanger, noise filter, inlet, coils/inductors, motion control, encoder, solenoid, digital/analog panel meters, printed circuit board, ic socket, camera link connector cable, slip ring rotary connectors, trimmer potentiometer, dial, knob, switch, fire and smoke detectors, peltier heater, room pressure control equipment, speaker, cables, wires, tube, screw springs, rack, solder, adhesive, anti-static, recording and abrasive paper, battery, touch panel, motherboard, switching HUB, HDMI, card slots, memory cards, measuring instrument, remote control, cleaning machine, rom burner, lubricants, pneumatic equipment, temperature regulator, heater, crimping machine and wire processing machine, shelves and storerooms, and other ICT equipment related. Its products are used in semiconductor/FPD manufacturing, measurement/semiconductor inspection, medical/beauty, ship, food/beverage/pharmaceutical inspection, logistics/transportation/packaging, printing, and other industrial equipment, as well as railway broadcasting systems, highway display boards, tunnel construction equipment, etc. The company was founded in 1967 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Adventure, Inc. operates an online travel reservation platform under the skyticket name. It offers air ticket booking, local tours, private lodging management, and offshore development services. Adventure, Inc. was formerly known as Cyber Travel Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Adventure Inc. in June 2013. Adventure, Inc. was founded in 2004 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

OYO Corporation provides geological survey services in Japan and internationally. It operates through three segments: Disaster Prevention and Infrastructure, Environment and Energy, and International. The Disaster Prevention and Infrastructure engages in surveying and restoration design of areas damaged by natural disasters; forecasting damage, such as earthquakes, tsunamis, fires, etc. related to national and local governments disaster prevention plans; consulting on natural disaster risk surveys, loss forecasting, and countermeasures related to corporate business continuity plans; and building, installing, and updating monitoring systems related to natural disasters, including seismometer networks and volcano monitoring systems. This segment also offers inspection, diagnosis, and maintenance services for social infrastructure; survey, design, and measurement services for the development of resilient infrastructure; develops, sells, constructs, installs, and renews non-destructive testing products used for inspections and diagnoses; and constructs, installs, and renews environmental monitoring systems. The Environment and Energy segment provides consulting on surveys and countermeasures focusing on soil and groundwater contamination, asbestos, waste, etc.; surveys and consulting for global warming countermeasures and biodiversity conservation; geological survey consulting related to the construction, installation, and renewal of environmental monitoring systems; methane hydrate development research; support for the commercialization of renewable energy; Undersea3D resource exploration services; and develops and sells resource exploration equipment and systems. The International segment develops, manufactures, and sells non-destructive testing equipment and seismic observation systems, geophysical exploration equipment; and offers survey, design, and construction management services. OYO Corporation was founded in 1954 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sakura Rubber Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells specialty rubber products. The company offers firefighting products, including fire hoses, firefighting suction hoses, toxic substances measuring instruments, firefighting training and rescue apparatus, air respirators, emergency life saving tools, powered impact tools, air lifting bags, community disaster prevention apparatus, couplings for large diameter hoses, MVU fans and blowers, and rescue support systems, as well as mountain, hydraulic, and water rescue tools. It also provides aerospace products, such as various fittings; PTFE, rubber, lightweight rubber, oxygen suction, and duct hoses; extruded, sheet, and molded goods; and inflatable seals, insulation products, bending tubes, and quick disconnectors. In addition, the company offers industrial rubber products comprising tank vapor seals; oil-resistant hoses for petrochemical plants; suction delivery hoses; tank lorry hoses; gate seals; joints; rubber sheets; pressed products; molded rubber; and insulation and hydraulic hoses, as well as couplings for the general industry. Further, it engages in the real estate business, such as operation of shopping malls. The company was incorporated in 1918 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

GSI Creos Corporation offers textiles and industrial products in Japan and internationally. It offers fibers for use in materials for innerwear, legwear, stockings, tights, and socks, sweaters, and bedding; recycled and biodegradable materials; natural fibers; elastic, finished, and functional yarns; plant-derived, thermal, semi-synthetic, and cooling fiber; and women, men, and children clothing, outdoor wear, and sportswear. It also engages in production, and sale of modeling paints, tools, plastic models, and products for handcrafts; selling household items and sanitary goods; insoles; e- commerce business; supply of spatial care, antibacterial, and antiviral masks, and bathtime tops; selling health food ingredients; and selling medical devices, including dialysis machines and equipment. In addition, it engages in the handling of cosmetic raw materials derived from plants, the ocean, microalgaeas; skin and haircare raw materials; and provides nail oils as nail-related products, including nail art brushes, nail oils, files, cleaners, washers, air nail system, reed diffuser, base gel, and nail balm. Further, it develops and sells products with cutting-edge technologies; machineries used for the manufacturing of industrial products, semiconductors, and medical devices; supplies silicon wafers, ceramic materials, and quartz parts; vaporizers, wafter transfer robots, and deformation measuring devices and particle coating; and engages in import, export, and sales of various industrial components. Additionally, it offers cleaning and glass parting agents; rubber hoses; papers; self-healing paints; fluorine and fluorine materials; electrical discharge, plastic molding, composite material-related, and textile machines; ion exchange resins; filters; materials; and equipment. The company was formerly known as Gunze Sangyo, Inc. and changed its name to GSI Creos Corporation in 2001. GSI Creos Corporation was incorporated in 1931 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Beet Sugar Manufacturing Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells functional products in Japan. The company offers beet and refined sugar; baker's yeast and oligosaccharides; compound and functional feed for dairy and beef cattle, as well as feed ingredient analysis and design, and farming support. It also manufactures and sells paper pot, soil filling and sowing tools, and transplanting equipment under the Chain Pot and Hipparikun names. In addition, the company is involved in the rental of office buildings and commercial stores; and research and development activities. Nippon Beet Sugar Manufacturing Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1919 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Ivy Cosmetics Corporation manufactures and sells cosmetics and beauty supplement products. The company provides penetrating beauty liquid, lotion/emulsion/cream, and cleansing/face wash products. It also offers optional care, makeup, hair care, and body care products; health food/equipment, UV protection, and men's cosmetics. The company was formerly known as Shirogane Corporation and changed its name to Ivy Cosmetics Corporation in October 1977. Ivy Cosmetics Corporation was incorporated in 1975 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Felt Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells press felts, shoe press belts, and industrial textile products in Japan, Asia, and internationally. It offers papermaking felts and pulp-making felts; felt for slate and building materials manufacturing; synthetic textiles; filter cloths, heat-resistant felts, sanforized blankets, felt for pleat-making machines, felt for conveyor belts, felts for waste treatment, etc.; industrial tubular needle-felts for industrial use; industrial woven jackets; industrial detergents and chemicals; and high-pressure shower equipment for papermaking. The company also engages in producing, finishing, and selling felt for making paper, pulp, slate, and other industrial applications; and industrial machinery and anti-pollution devices, as well as leasing and managing of real estate. Nippon Felt Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1917 and is headquartered in Kita, Japan.

Techno Alpha Co., Ltd. operates as a trading company in Japan and internationally. The company imports semiconductor manufacturing equipment, as well as supplies equipment, such as wedge wire bonder, bond tester, vacuum reflow soldering, VPS reflow, bonding tool, the microfocus x-ray system, plasma cleaner, die bonder, flip chip bonder products. The company also sells marine machinery and filtration products, including marine cranes, life boats, and boat davits; equipment for food and chemistry to be separated and concentrated with vibrating membrane systems; and solar cell analytical instruments and X-ray inspection systems. In addition, it operates as a system integrator; supplies measurement and inspection systems; develops and produces biochemistry instruments, which include nanoLC pumps and DiNa Series products that are used in the proteomics field; and imports and exports various biochemistry devices. Further, the company provides surface mount technology equipment/materials; LabVIEW solutions; organic photo-detector, image sensor, PDV system ferroelectric material, and Silber nano ink products; automatic shoe sole cleaners, cyclone suction mats, automatic caster cleaners, and all touchless multi-biometric access systems; injection plunger tip and material packaging tear tape products; filtration products; and educational services. Additionally, it offers electronic circuit printer, topological material, cutter, EMI tester, real-time AMC monitoring system, adhesive glue, and polarizer products; chemical science products; and temperature monitoring and fiber optic photometer products, and temperature monitoring systems. The company was incorporated in 1989 and is headquartered in Shinagawa, Japan.

Neturen Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells steel bars in Japan and internationally. It operates through Specialty Steel and Wire Products, Induction Heating, and Others segments. The company offers prestressed concrete (PC) steel bars, deformed PC steel bars, and shear reinforcement products for civil engineering and construction applications; high-strength spring steel wire (ITW) products for the use in suspension springs for automobiles and two-wheeled vehicles; automotive parts; construction machine parts; machining equipment; and pre-hardened wires. It is also involved in the manufacturing and sale of induction heating equipment and high-frequency hardening equipment; contract processing, manufacturing, and sale of high-frequency quenching of machine parts, etc.; provision of induction heat treatment services for critical safety parts of automobiles and machining equipment, etc.; and development of technical products business. In addition, the company leases movables and real estate properties. Neturen Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1940 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

First-corporation Inc. operates as a general construction company in Japan. It operates through Construction Business and Real Estate Business segments. The company constructs condominiums and provides services to condominium developers. It also engages in the buying, selling, and brokerage of land and buildings; and operating as a sales agency for newly built condominiums. First-corporation Inc. was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Suginami, Japan.

GOLDCREST Co.,Ltd. plans, develops, and sells new condominiums in Japan. It is also involved in the real estate leasing and management, and hotel businesses. The company was incorporated in 1968 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

DKK Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of telecommunication and radio frequency equipment in Japan and internationally. The company's telecommunication equipment include mobile communication systems comprising cellular base station antennas, wireless equipment, small antennas, peripheral devices, and mobile communication towers, as well as provides cellular base station construction and maintenance services; and broadcasting products, such as broadcasting and microwave antennas, medium and short wave antennas, steel towers, shelters, power supply apparatus, and FPU rotators, as well as provides broadcasting construction and maintenance services. It also offers digital disaster prevention administrative radio/fire and emergency digital radio, multi-channel access, micro line antenna, and satellite communication products; high-altitude monitoring cameras, tsunami evacuation towers, disaster prevention information network systems, and telescopic columns, as well as maintenance services; CATV construction and telescopic masts; LED aviation obstacle lights, wind power towers, marine beacon, and fuel cells; and engages in volcano, fire detection, tsunami, river, and high-altitude monitoring systems; and defense related business, as well as thermal camera and software lineups, and AI automatic detection system. In addition, the company provides induction heating; high frequency oscillators; IoT technology and waveform monitor equipment; heating coils; RF applied equipment; and vacuum furnace. The company was founded in 1925 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Dai-Ichi Cutter Kogyo k.k. engages in the construction and civil engineering activities in Japan. The company offers flat sawing, core drilling, wall and wire sawing, grooving, various dry cutting and drilling, and various metal cutting; concrete chipping, concrete surface treatment, metal cutting, and plant equipment cleaning decontamination services; shot and vacuum blast and decontamination services; and various cleaning, polishing, mechanical cutting, peeling, and mirror floor polishing services. It also provides building maintenance services comprising drain pipe and cleaning/inspection/construction of water supply and drainage equipment, rainwater pipe, tank and side gutter cleaning, spring tank, water tank cleaning/endoscopic investigation, and mechanical parking lot pit services. In addition, the company offers anchor construction, burster construction, industrial waste collection, and transportation/intermediate treatment services. Further, it engages in repair of bridge, port construction, and dam-related construction; and renewal work comprising demolition work, seismic work, and other construction services, as well as provides engineering services for railway. Dai-Ichi Cutter Kogyo k.k. was incorporated in 1967 and is based in Chigasaki, Japan.

Systems Design Co., Ltd. operates as an information service company. The company' system development business offers range of services comprising system development, operation, and maintenance solutions; infrastructure construction, operation, and maintenance solutions; big data solutions; easy system development and maintenance servicing using framework, system development and maintenance service using workflow, and business visualization services; and data analysis, cloud infrastructure construction, RPA business automation support, and telework environment introduction support services, as well as knowledge search AI services. It also engages in the outsourcing business activities, including BPO, one-stop, PC lifecycle, contact center, product, and document management services, as well as digitalization of specific health checkups and guidance, data entry and library services. The company was incorporated in 1967 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

MRT Inc. operates a medical information platform. It provides Gaikin, a recruitment service specializing in part-time physicians; career, a job change support service for doctors; re:ray, a one stop outsourcing service; Door., an app to connect network of doctors; Door.into, an app platform that connects doctors and medical institutions with individuals; Pocket Doctor, a telemedicine app; groupware for medical departments; and FASTCALL a safety confirmation service for medical institutions consolidating the functions for hospital crisis management systems. The company also offers stem cell culture supernatant for medical institutions; pharmacist, clinical engineering technician, radiologic technologists, dialysis nurses, new practice openings and succession cases, clinical laboratory technician, and nurse recruitment services; job posting service for full-time and part-time industrial physicians; occupational health services; employment placement services; life support services; and full-time, part-time, and temporary positions for healthcare and welfare professionals. In addition, it engages in book publishing on illness and treatment; medical worker job placement business; business start-up; business succession support business; PR services, comprehensive membership compensation system, tax accountant referral services, and human umbilical cord-derived culture supernatant. Further, it offers anew, a service aimed at payroll calculation for doctors part-time jobs and payroll transfer agency services; Medikiki, an information distribution platform connecting manufacturers and wholesalers; and Kids Line that provides babysitting services. MRT Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Mitsuboshi Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells electric wires and synthetic resin products in Japan. The company offers electric wires, such as rubber flexible cables, welding cables, vinyl flexible cables, Star MIC cable series, flexible cords, and high-performance tubes, including thermoflon, blacktrack, rumflon series, transformer master series, popurex series tubes that are applied in physical and chemical equipment, analytical equipment, semiconductor processing, chemical industry, electronic industry, general industry, and other applications; and heating wires used in home appliances, industrial and food furnaces, fittings, industrial equipment, and automotive applications. The company was formerly known as Mitsuboshi Trading Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Mitsuboshi Co., Ltd. in 1971. Mitsuboshi Co., Ltd. was founded in 1919 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

IID, Inc. engages in the media, research, and media commerce businesses in Japan. It operates through Creator platform business and Creator solution business segments. The company operates various media centered and news websites comprising business, practical, hobbies, information technology, automobiles, education, movies, games, anime, fashion, and other media content. It also offers content and research solutions and media consulting to customers, as well as quantitative and qualitative surveys; and shop management ASP systems for EC businesses. In addition, it engages in publishing and online advertising business. The company was formerly known as IRI Commerce & Technology Inc. and changed its name to IID, Inc. in June 2010. IID, Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Seiki Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the manufacture and sale of instruments for automobiles, motorcycles, agricultural and construction machines, and marine instruments in Japan, the Americas, Europe, and rest of Asia. The company provides instrument cluster, head-up displays; level, revolution, angle, stroke, inertial, and ultrasonic velocity sensors; automotive EMS with high-density PCB mounting for automotive components; and aftermarket products, such as link meters and multi-digital displays under the Defi brand; and plastic optics design and manufacturing services. It also offers household appliances, including remote controllers for air conditioners, water heater, and central controller equipment; office automation equipment comprising operation panels for multi-functional printers; and factory automation equipment for remote terminal. In addition, the company sells new and used automobiles; and provides car inspection and repair services, as well as offers car rental services. Further, it engages in the process and sale of resin materials; manufactures speedometers and engine tachometers, building and household control circuits, assemble harnesses for in-vehicle instruments, and fuel gauge sender units; provision of logistics, import/ export customs clearance, food, insurance agency, and advertising agency services; and information systems and services, and software and UI consulting business. Nippon Seiki Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1946 and is headquartered in Nagaoka, Japan.

Fujii Sangyo Corporation engages in the sale of electrical construction materials, electrical equipment, machine tools, information equipment, and civil engineering and construction machinery in Japan. It operates through Material Innovations Company, Infrastructure Solutions Company, Komatsu Construction Equipment Sales and Service Japan, Ltd., and Others segments. The company distributes lighting fixtures, electric wires, distribution boards, air conditioners, ventilation fans, wiring and electrical circuit equipment, communication and video equipment, audio equipment, solar power generation systems, and power storage equipment; computer equipment and office supplies; video and audio equipment, network equipment, surveillance and security equipment, meeting hall equipment, electronic medical records and nurse call equipment, and IP telephone and CATV equipment; and construction and plant equipment. It also constructs and sells exterior wall materials, roofing materials, foundation structural materials, and civil engineering materials; develops and sells information processing systems and software; designs, constructs, and maintains information-related equipment; and designs and constructs industrial buildings and gas facilities. In addition, the company sells control and power distribution equipment, electronic devices, semiconductors, environmental equipment, various production materials, industrial robots, various machine tools, machining centers, and press machines; and designs and manufactures automatic control panels and automated equipment. Further, it engages in the provision of lighting design, energy conservation support, and consulting; sale, maintenance, and rental of civil engineering and construction machinery, and its parts; product repair and after-sale services; and solar power generation activities, including ground and roof installation. Fujii Sangyo Corporation was founded in 1883 and is headquartered in Utsunomiya, Japan.

EIZO Corporation designs, develops, manufactures, and sells visual display systems, amusement monitors, and imaging system software in Japan and internationally. It provides LCD, color management, and home entertainment monitors; operating room solutions; and medical monitor solutions. The company also offers industrial monitors; and ATC visual display solutions, such as primary control monitors, tower monitors, encoding and streaming solutions, data capture and content management solutions, and graphics boards, as well as other accessories. In addition, it provides business enterprise, healthcare, creative work, security and surveillance, touch panel, maritime, air traffic control, and home entertainment solutions. The company was formerly known as Eizo Nanao Corporation and changed its name to EIZO Corporation in 2013. The company was incorporated in 1968 and is headquartered in Hakusan, Japan.

Ribomic Inc. discovers and develops pharmaceutical compounds using RNA aptamers in Japan. The company develops drugs for wet age-related macular degeneration, achondroplasia, pulmonary arterial hypertension, proliferative vitreoretinopathy, heart failure, osteoarthritis, and asthma, as well as separating agents for affinity purification of human IgG and Fc-fusion proteins. The company was incorporated in 2003 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

MK Seiko Co., Ltd. develops, produces, and sells automotive service and information equipment, and household products. It offers car washing machines, car tire air filling machines, automatic car cleaning machines, car mat washers, fluid and oil related equipment, air conditioner related equipment, kerosene delivery lorries, pumps, and coolant cleaning machines, as well as LED display for gas stations, stores, construction, public demand information boards, digital signages, and LLC related equipment. The company also provides cooking appliances; agricultural materials; storage items; and audio equipment, as well as electric fuel pumps. MK Seiko Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1956 and is headquartered in Chikuma, Japan.

Sanko Sangyo Co.,Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of adhesive and adhesive-backed printed materials in Japan. Its products are used in industrial products, including seals, labels, and stickers, as well as electronic devices; and display boards for office equipment, control panels, display windows for digital cameras and mobile phones, and automotive equipment. The company also provides a range of printed materials comprising general commercial printed materials, cards, POP displays, promotional items, and original goods. Sanko Sangyo Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1951 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Daikoku Denki Co., Ltd. development, production, and sale of computer systems pachinko parlors in Japan. The company also develops, manufactures, and sells units for pachinko and pachislo game machines. In addition, it provides Daikoku Denki Strategic Information System, a service system to help pachinko hall operators formulate management strategies; and information on pachinko halls and game machines through PC and cell-phone Web sites, and pay TV channels. Daikoku Denki Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Nagoya, Japan.

Ricksoft Co., Ltd. engages in the provision of IT services in Japan. It offers Professional Services, such as research and analysis, design, construction, operation, and application; Step-Up Agile with Jira, an enterprise Agile services; D-Accel platform; Cadre, which connects Jira software and confluence data to a BI tool; and Tableau, a visual analytics platform. The company was incorporated in 2005 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Akatsuki Inc. engages in the entertainment, lifestyle, and solutions businesses in Japan. The company operates through two segments, Games Business and Comics Business. It plans, develops, and operates games, including Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle and Romancing SaGa Re;univerSe. The company is also involved in planning, production, and distribution of full-color vertical comics; and operation of the HykeComic app. In addition, it offers Slash Gift, an online lottery service. The company was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Tokuden Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells welding materials in Japan. The company offers flux-cored wires for submerged arc welding and MAG welding, arc welding electrodes for hard facing, wire rods for TIG and MIG welding, agglomerated and fused fluxes, powdered material for PTA welding, etc. It also undertakes welding construction works, such as various types of welding and overhaul works to iron-making, petrochemicals, cement, home electronics, automobile, and foodstuff industries; and provides pipes, top plates, iron foundry press-equalizing devices, trunnion journals, and chutes, as well as environment-related equipment, including deodorizing devices and forced-cooling apparatus. Tokuden Co., Ltd. was founded in 1933 and is headquartered in Amagasaki, Japan.

The Yonkyu Co.,Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, operates in the fishery industry in Japan. It operates through the Fresh Fish Sales Business, Food and Feed Sales Business, and Other segments. The company engages in the sale of sea bream, yellowtail, and amberjack; artificial hatching of fish primarily red sea breams; raising, processing, and shipping of pacific bluefin tuna; and eel farming business. It also sells live feed, mixed feed, moist pellets. In addition, the company is involved in the general freight transportation business. The Yonkyu Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1961 and is headquartered in Uwajima, Japan.

TESEC Corporation designs, develops, manufactures, and sells semiconductor test equipment in Japan and internationally. It offers various test systems, including prober-integrated, power device, IPD/IPM, and dynamic test system, as well as thermal resistance, and inductive load tester. The company provides various handlers comprising micro electromechanical systems, film frame test, gravity, MEMS, and TAB handlers, as well as die sorters. In addition, it offers after-sales services, such as tester calibration, overhaul, and training services, as well as spare parts, warranty, maintenance, and resale services. The company was formerly known as TES Corporation and changed its name to TESEC Corporation in 1980. TESEC Corporation was incorporated in 1969 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Art Vivant Co., Ltd. operates in the art business. The company sells paintings, including printed, oil painting, and watercolor products; and arts and crafts, such as sculptures and glass crafts, as well as miscellaneous art goods. It also engages in yoga / fitness and finance business. The company provides credit products; and operates resort and fitness club, as well as Amida for lava hot yoga. Art Vivant Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1971 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Dainichiseika Color & Chemicals Mfg. Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells powders, pigment dispersions, and functional materials. It offers inorganic, organic, and prepared pigments for applications including paints, printing inks, and information display and recording materials; mass colorants for synthetic fibers, pigment printing agents, and colorants for paper and construction materials; and colorants for thermoplastic resins. The company also provides colorants for PVC, fluoropolymer, and other resins; UV and EB curable coating agents and decorative and functional coating agents, as well as functional products for the electronics and information materials, automotive and interior construction materials fields; and urethane resins and colorants used for synthetic leather and molded products, special coating agents and adhesives. In addition, it offers Chitosan polymer and its derivatives; printing inks for paper media; and inks and peripheral products for gravure printing. The company was founded in 1931 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Kyowa Engineering Consultants Co., Ltd. provides construction consultancy services in Japan and internationally. It operates through the Construction Consulting Business, Information Processing Business, and Real Estate Rental and Management Business segments. The company offers structure design and maintenance services, such as survey, planning, and design of bridges, underground structures, water and sewage facilities, tunnels, utility tunnels, etc.; inspection and repair technologies; construction management. It also provides regional development and creation services, including urban development, surveys, planning, and design of roads, parks, green spaces, and land development; surveys, predictions, and countermeasures for the social and natural environment; landscape studies. In addition, the company offers disaster prevention and mitigation services for investigation, planning, and design of rivers, waterways, sluice gates, and related facilities; erosion control-related investigations and planning; geotechnical and hydrological investigations and analyses, as well as surveys of energy reserves, including solar, hydro, wind, and biomass, implementation planning and design, regional utilization plans, and consensus building support; architecture services, such as seismic diagnosis of buildings, seismic design, and improvement of the asset value of building facilities, as well as investigation and design of drinking water development projects, construction supervision and support for non-revenue water countermeasures, and support for renewable energy development; and promoting small-scale hydropower generation and supporting regional revitalization. Additionally, the company engages in real estate rental and management; information processing; personnel dispatch; sale of information processing equipment; and software development activities. It serves government agencies. The company was incorporated in 1961 and is headquartered in Shibuya, Japan.

Maruyama Mfg. Co., Inc. manufactures and sells machinery in Japan and internationally. The company offers agricultural machines, such as brush cutters, furrow trimmers, riding and self-propelled grass cutters, power sprayers, backpack portable power sprayers, manual pest control machines, power spreaders, ditch cutters, aerial and electric work platforms, and rice related products; riding and large pest control machines, including stereo and boom sprayers, high creation boom, multirotor for liquid spraying, multi and shuttle sprayers, and freshhousers; green related products, comprising chainsaws, hedge trimmers, blowers, chippers, and shredders; pumps, cleaning machines, and mist generators, such as pump motor sets, industrial pumps, high pressure washers, and disinfection equipment; and fire extinguishers and disaster prevention equipment. It also provides ultra fine bubble related products, which includes ultra pumps, washing machines, ultra coolant freshers, mounted generators, and general water supply installation; power spreaders; agricultural drones; and engine pumps and cleaners. In addition, the company involved in the contract design, construction of plumbing, and real estate and leasing activities. It was formerly known as Maruyama Shokai and changed its name to Maruyama Mfg. Co., Inc. in 1937. Maruyama Mfg. Co., Inc. was founded in 1895 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

BIKE O & COMPANY Ltd. retails used motorcycles in Japan. The company also engages in the sale of new motorcycles wholesale to motorcycle retail stores and other dealers via auctions for dealers, and public through stores and online sales via the internet; export of vehicles; and sale of used motorcycle parts and accessories; used cars; sale of e-bike through e-commerce website and physical store. In addition, the company provides vehicle liability and optional insurance; and vehicle coating services. Further, it is involved in rental motorcycle business, and sells electric mobility. The company sells its products under the BIKE O brand through retail stores and e-commerce website. BIKE O & COMPANY Ltd. was founded in 1994 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Harima-Kyowa Co.,LTD. engages in the wholesale of daily necessities, cosmetics, and toiletries in Japan. The company offers detergent-related, home product, household paper, stone testing, sanitary products, nursing care products, cosmetics, oil processing, paper and processed paper, packaging materials and hygiene products. It also provides logistics services and warehousing services. The company distributes its products to stores, including drug stores, supermarkets, and home improvement stores. In addition, it is involved in the leasing of real estate properties. Harima-Kyowa Co.,LTD. was founded in 1907 and is headquartered in Himeji, Japan.

J-Oil Mills, Inc. manufacture and sell vegetable oils and fats in Japan. The company operates through Oils and Fats Business and Specialty Foods Business segments. It is involved in manufacturing, processing, and sales of oils and fats, fats and oil cakes, starch, feed and fertilizer, and other various foods; cultivation, development, processing, and sales of agricultural products; sale of food manufacturing equipment; and real estate rental business. The company also offers margarine, shortening, oilseed meal, and proteins. J-Oil Mills, Inc. was founded in 1826 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Daiho Corporation engages in the civil engineering and construction business in Japan and internationally. The company is involved in the design, supervision, and consultation of civil engineering and construction works; research, survey, and design of hydroelectric power generation; design and construction using pneumatic caisson method; design, manufacture, repair, sale, and lease of construction machinery; real estate sales, leasing, management, and brokerage; and research, design, and consultation related to regional and urban development, and other land use. It also provides environmental restoration services, including soil and water purification; industrial waste collection, transportation, treatment, and reuse services; and consulting services. Daiho Corporation was incorporated in 1949 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

OPEN Group, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a technology company. The company operates through Intellectual Property, Robot Transformation Business, and RaaS Business segments. It offers BizRobo, a software robot that performs various routine tasks; PRESCO, a performance-based advertising service; and RoboRobo, a cloud-based back office efficiency tools. The company engages in the sales outsourcing business. The company was formerly known as RPA Holdings, Inc. and changed its name to OPEN Group, Inc. in June 2024. OPEN Group, Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Inabata & Co.,Ltd. engages in information and electronics, chemicals, life industry, and plastics businesses in Japan and internationally. The company offers polarizing films, materials for polarizing films, alignment layer materials, color resist, material for backlights and OLEDs, surface treatment equipment and films, driver ICs, led encapsulants, materials for non-woven mask and special-use tape for display, automotive-related films, sensors, and various materials for power semiconductors. It also provides materials and equipment for the display and semiconductor domains, including semiconductor manufacturing equipment and mask blanks; develop and manufacture plastic for LED encapsulants in-house, as well as plastics for display encapsulants, heat dissipation plastics, and high-refractive-index resins; and supplies materials and equipment for displays and semiconductors, materials for advanced semiconductor substrates, and power semiconductor wafers. In addition, the company offers synthetic resin and fiber materials, catalysts, antioxidants, ultraviolet absorbents, solvents, polymerization initiators, functional resins, silicone, silane products, health care-related products, materials for data recording papers and polyurethane form, products for pipelines, and inorganic materials for public infrastructure and civil engineering related products; coating materials; sell materials for automobile parts and chemicals, as well as materials and products for housing, housing material, and housing equipment manufacturers; import and sell wooden materials; and businesses for wood and housing materials. Further, it sells plastics, molded plastic products, and machinery; supplies plastic materials; solutions for plastic materials, logistics, and financing for manufacturing equipment; and offers molded products. The company was founded in 1890 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Nakakita Seisakusho Co., Ltd. designs, produces, and sells automatic control valves, butterfly valves, and remote control systems. Its control/shut-off valves include self operated type regulating, auxiliary power operated type control, remotely operated, cylinder, and safety valves, as well as pneumatic operated automatic controllers, pneumatic operated actuators, and nuclear power plant valves; and butterfly valves comprise manually operated, remote-control, and cryogenic type butterfly valves. The company also engages in the installation of pressure reducing and desuperheater other automatic control valves, and equipment. In addition, it offers valve remote control systems for cargo and ballast line and level gauging systems; and various systems for ship engines, such as fuel oil heating, fresh water cooling, and lubricating oil cooling systems; and automatic control valves for use in gas turbine power plants. Nakakita Seisakusho Co., Ltd. was founded in 1930 and is headquartered in Daito, Japan.

Gun Ei Chemical Industry Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells synthetic resins and fibers in Japan and internationally. It operates through three segments: Chemicals Business, Food Business, Real Estate Utilization Business. The company offers RESITOP for photoresist, reactive polyimide, phenolic resins, and high purity bisphenol F, which are used in electronic materials; Resitop for shell molding; NFURAN, which is used for curing of furan resin by acids as catalyst; a-system, a self-curing mold production system; and AR SAND that offers sand filling efficiency and mold strength. It also provides functional phenolic resin, kynol novoloid fibers, spherical phenolic resins, s-RP nano sand, high purity bisphenol F, and Milex special phenolic resin that are used in industrial materials. In addition, the company provides foods, such as starches and sugars; and business outsourcing and staffing services. Further, it involved in the leasing of real estate. Gun Ei Chemical Industry Co., Ltd. was founded in 1946 and is headquartered in Takasaki, Japan.

Inbound Tech Inc. engages in the Multilingual CRM and sales outsourcing businesses in Japan. The company offers multilingual contact center services, including help desk and system monitoring services; multilingual/interpretation solutions, such as telephone, AI video, and three-way interpretations that support inbound foreigners in various situations; and contact center BPO services, as well as specialized sales outsourcing services. It also engages in mobile promotion-related business. The company offers services in Japanese, English, Chinese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish, Thai, Vietnamese, Russian, French, Tagalog, and Nepali languages. It serves clients across various industries, including multinational corporations, foreign-affiliated companies, and consumer goods and services sectors. The company was formerly known as Brainpress Inc. Inbound Tech Inc. was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in Shinjuku, Japan.

Chiyoda Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the development, manufacture, and sale of shoes in Japan. It is also involved in the purchase and wholesale of shoes; and import of shoes and garments. The company operates stores under SHOE PLAZA, TOKYO SHOES RETAILING CENTER, CHIYODA HAKI-GOKOCHI, Chiyoda, cloverleaf, SPC, VifVif by cloverleaf, COURIPIE, and NATURA brand names. Chiyoda Co., Ltd. was founded in 1936 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Kyowanissei Co.,Ltd. engages in the construction and civil engineering businesses in Japan. It operates in four segments: Gas Equipment Business, Gas Pipeline Business, Building Equipment Business, and Electrical and Civil Engineering Business. The company offers gas and appliance installation services, including design and installation of gas piping equipment; heating; hot water supply and air conditioning equipment construction; solar cell, fuel cell, and battery system construction; repair of commercial air conditioners; maintenance and inspection of commercial air conditioners; and commercial air conditioner cleaning services. It also provides gas pipeline construction, such as gas pipeline laying work; road paving work related to gas pipeline installation work; and maintenance services for gas pipeline and gas leakage. In addition, the company offers facility construction services comprising water supply and drainage sanitary facilities; heating, cooling, and ventilation construction; sale and construction of gas heat pump air conditioners and gas hot water systems; construction of disaster prevention equipment, which includes sprinkler equipment and inert gas fire extinguishing equipment; construction management of various piping facilities; renewal and rehabilitation works; construction of drainage and water supply pipes rehabilitation; and maintenance of water supply, hot water supply, drainage, ventilation, and other facilities. Further, it provides electrical installation and maintenance; construction of irrigation systems for golf courses, water supply and drainage systems for clubhouses, and air conditioning systems, as well as irrigation and greening of soccer stadiums and school grounds; general civil engineering works; road pavement repair work and parking lot maintenance works; and sewer pipe connections installation. The company was founded in 1893 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Onamba Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells general-purpose wires, communication cables, consumer electronic equipment, information office equipment, and industrial electronic equipment in Japan, rest of Asia, Europe, and America. It offers wires and cables for machine internal; coaxial cables; cables for solar power generation, fixed, and moving applications; environmentally friendly cables; and harnesses for automobile, industrial equipment, and consumer products. The company also provides renewable energy resources products, including PVU-Finder, a solar power generation intelligent monitoring and control system; and E&E-Solution, a self-consumption integrated system. In addition, it offers junction boxes and cables for solar power generation systems, as well as branch boxes/extension and crossing cables; connectors for automobiles; and harness processing equipment and machinery. Onamba Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1941 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Santo Corporation engages in the construction contracting business in Japan. The company provides general civil engineering works, such as erosion and flood control works, dam/river construction works, road/bridge construction works, land preparation works, golf course construction works, agricultural civil engineering works, dredging works, ground improvement works, water and sewage works, land development works, port/airport related works, and road works; paving works for land, roads, etc.; special greening works; and underground engineering services. It also undertakes general construction works, including hotels, office/government buildings, factories, stores, warehouse/distribution facilities, apartments/residences, educational and cultural facilities, medical/welfare facilities, temples and shrines, schools/hospitals, amusement facilities, reforms, as well as rental/condominium construction works. In addition, the company provides environmental planning, research, design, supervision, construction, and operation, as well as real estate sales, rentals, brokerages, management, and appraisals. Further, it is involved in the development of land and buildings. Santo Corporation was incorporated in 1954 and is headquartered in Ritto, Japan.

Townnews-Sha Co.,Ltd. engages in the publishing business in Japan. It issues Town News, a local information paper and selling advertising space on local politics, economy, society, culture, sports, and other topics. It also publishes commemorative magazines, various printed materials, and engages in operation of website and video production. In addition, it provides Political village, database of politicians in the newspaper's publishing area; RareA, neighborhood information site; and Town News for LINE, distribution service. Further, it engages in the regional production and public private partnership projects. Townnews-Sha Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1977 and is headquartered in Yokohama, Japan.

Mortgage Service Japan Limited engages in mortgage lending and brokerage business. The company operates through Housing Finance Business, Home Defect Insurance and Other Business, and Housing Academia Business segments. It is also involved in the provision of housing loans; life insurance and intermediary for conclusion of life insurance contracts; non-life insurance agency business; and education, publishing, consulting, etc. in the field of housing loans. Mortgage Service Japan Limited was incorporated in 2005 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Aval Data Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells industrial electronic equipment in Japan. The company offers general communication equipment, industrial measuring instruments and control equipment, applied electronic equipment, and electronic equipment for physics, chemistry, medicine, and others. It also provides image input board, image processing device, near-infrared camera, software, and accessories; high-speed optical communication board and matrix switch; A/D conversion board; CPU/MPU and wireless modules. In addition, the company offers contract development and product customization services. Aval Data Corporation was incorporated in 1959 and is headquartered in Machida, Japan.

Akatsuki Corp., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the real estate and securities businesses in Japan. It plans, designs, constructs, and revitalizes used real estate properties, as well as sells renovated and furnished apartments. The company also develops paid nursing homes; and trunk room rental services. In addition, it is involved in the architectural design and supervision. The company was formerly known as Akatsuki Financial Group, Inc. and changed its name to Akatsuki Corp. in July 2016. Akatsuki Corp. was incorporated in 1950 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Sankyo Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells game machines and ball bearing supply systems in Japan. It operates through Pachinko Machines Business, Pachislot Machines Business, Ball Bearing Supply Systems Business, and Other Businesses segments. The Pachinko Machines Business segment produces and sells pachinko machines, machine gauges, and related parts. The Pachinko Machines Business segment produces and sells pachislot machines and sells related parts. The Supply Equipment Related Business segment produces and sells pachinko and pachislot replenishment device, card system equipment, and hall equipment peripherals. The Other Businesses segment engages in real estate rental and sale of general molded parts. The company also offers mobile content services and operates a golf club. It sells its products under the SANKYO, Bisty, and JB brands. Sankyo Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1960 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Oriental Shiraishi Corporation produces, constructs, and sells prestressed concrete products and structures in Japan. It operates through three segments: Construction Business, Steel Structure Business, and Port Business. The Construction business segment engages in construction work and manufacturing/sales of prestressed concrete civil engineering structures, pneumatic caissons; repair and reinforcement work for bridges, prestressed concrete building structures; and design and construction, design and production of construction equipment, civil engineering contracting, and rental of construction machinery. The Steel Structure Business segment involved in the design, manufacturing, erection, repair and reinforcement of steel structures such as bridges. The Port business segment operates port, civil engineering, and construction work. It also engages in the design and construction of earthquake-resistant buildings; and the sale of construction materials and devices. In addition, the company generates, supplies, and sells solar power through the operation and administration of solar power plants. Oriental Shiraishi Corporation was incorporated in 1952 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Sanko Metal Industrial Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture, process, execution, and sale of roofing products, construction materials, and paint products in Japan. It also designs and contracts ancillary construction works. The company was incorporated in 1949 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Semba Corporation engages in the planning, designing, supervision, and construction of commercial facilities and interiors in Japan, Taiwan, and internationally. The company offers research and analysis, concept creation, planning and consulting, design and planning, production and construction, spatial design utilizing digital technology, maintenance, and facility management services. Its services are used for various fields, including shopping malls and restaurants, offices, educational facilities, healthcare facilities, hotels, and leisure facilities. Semba Corporation was founded in 1947 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Collabos Corporation develops and provides cloud-based call center systems. It offers nyplace, an IP telephone exchange service; COLLABOS PHONE, an IP-PBX/CTI system developed; GROWCE, a CRM and marketing service; COLLABOS CRM, inbound CRM service; COLLABOS CRM Outbound Edition, an outbound CRM service; Packet Folder, a call recording service; AmiVoice Communication Suite, a voice recognition service; Afullect, an overflow call collection service; GOLDEN LIST, a data analysis service; VLOOM, an IP-PBX/CTI system AI technology; and CollasQ, a FAQ service. The company was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Enjin Co., Ltd. provides public relation (PR) support services for small and medium sized enterprises and business owners in Japan. It operates through two segments, PR Consulting Services and Media Platform Services. The company plans and operates Medichoku, a media matching service that connects media with companies and medical institutions. It also provides PR consulting services; and supports investments in venture companies. The company was formerly known as Yadomaru Enjin Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Enjin Co., Ltd. in April 2009. Enjin Co., Ltd. was founded in 2006 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Yodoko, Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sales of steel products for industrial and consumer products in Japan. The company offers steel sheets products comprising pre-painted and galvanized steel sheets, chromate-free treated steel sheets, cold rolled steel strips, special steel strips, steel strapping, and cold rolled steel sheets. It also provides exterior products for storage sheds, garages, carports, bicycle parks, warehouses, lodges, and trash bins; and building materials comprising roofing and siding, and environmental products. In addition, the company offers rolls for section steel, including plate and hot strip mill work rolls; paper manufacturing rolls; drilled rolls for rubber and vinyl; and calendar equipment for paper making. Further, it provides high-function grating products, as well as other gratings products, including grating, and pit and U-shaped covers. Additionally, the company is involved in the real estate which operates buildings and parking lots for rent. Yodoko, Ltd. was formerly known as Yodogawa Steel Works, Ltd. and changed its name to Yodoko, Ltd. in October 2025. The company was incorporated in 1935 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Helios Techno Holding Co., Ltd. engages in the lamp and manufacturing equipment businesses in Japan. The company provides lamps for projectors, halogen lamps for general lighting, and LED lamps; equipment, such as orientation film printing devices and specialized printers; and industrial equipment. It is also involved in testing and measuring equipment. The company was formerly known as PHOENIX Electric Co., Ltd and changed its name to Helios Techno Holding Co., Ltd. in April 2009. The company was incorporated in 1976 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Miroku Corporation, through its subsidiaries, manufactures, purchases and sells hunting guns, machine tools, cloud solutions, and other products in Japan, the United States, Belgium, and internationally. The company offers firearms, wooden gun stocks, and component parts, as well as sporting and hunting guns under the Browning and Miroku brands. It also manufactures and sells machine tools, including gun drilling machines; and automotive parts comprising solid-wood steering wheels. In addition, the company provides deep-hole drilling service; and operates a workshop for ornamental engravings for firearms and other metal items. The company was founded in 1893 and is headquartered in Nankoku, Japan.

BeMap, Inc. engages in the mobile system integration business in Japan. The company operates through Mobility Innovation, Wireless Innovation, and Solution Business segments. It offers systems for railways, including route search services, as well as allows railway service companies to provide users with information using content management system (CMS) Wi-Fi infrastructures. The company also develops and maintains CMS for Wi-Fi; and Wi-Fi authentication systems that supports public wireless LAN services for telecommunication carriers. In addition, it provides systems for individuals to use a public wireless LAN at railway stations, airports, convenience stores, restaurants, and other places through telecommunication carriers and railway operators; and cloud-based digital video recording services. Further, the company offers 02020, an on-air to online to offline solution; TV metadata services; MMS business, content print business; and website/software development services. BeMap, Inc. was incorporated in 1998 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

SIIX Corporation primarily sells and distributes electronic components in Japan, China, the rest of Asia, Europe, and the Americas. The company offers automotive electronic components, such as automotive exteriors, meters, ECU, switches, quasi-millimeter wave radars, ABS, cameras, battery sensors, car multimedia, etc., as well as industrial equipment, such as engine ignition devices for power tools, battery packs, aircraft-borne electronic devices, inverters, medical devices, smart meters, industrial sewing machines, etc. It also provides home appliances, such as digital appliances, air conditioners, refrigerators, educational toys, healthcare equipment, electronic musical instruments, etc.; information devices, including office equipment, OA equipment, projectors, etc.; and automobile wire harness, motorcycles, inks, and other electronic components, etc. In addition, the company is involved in plastic molding and die manufacturing, and circuit board mounting, as well as provision of materials procurement, assembly, and processing of devices and components; and logistics services. SIIX Corporation was founded in 1957 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Eslead Corporation develops and sells family-type and urban condominiums in Japan. It also reconstructs and sells condominiums; develops and sells detached houses; and manages condominiums and buildings. In addition, the company undertakes new construction, extension and renovation, repair, commercial facility renewal, house renovation, model room construction, demolition works, etc.; rental apartment management activities; and contracts for high-voltage power. Further, it offers support services for real estate replacement, sale, and appraisal; hospitality services; digital marketing services; cleaning services; and general building maintenance services. Eslead Corporation was formerly known as Nihon Eslead Corporation and changed its name to Eslead Corporation in October 2019. The company was incorporated in 1968 and is based in Osaka, Japan. Eslead Corporation operates as a subsidiary of Mori Trust Co., Ltd.

KNT-CT Holdings Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides travel related services in Japan. The company is involved in planning and sales of travel products, tours, and overseas group tours; media sales through newspaper advertisements; and the distribution of the information magazine Tabi no Tomo, as well as web sales. It also engages in consignment business; temporary staffing, office work, and commercial business for inns and hotels; domestic and overseas group travel and individual travel services; advertising production businesses; assistance businesses; overseas airline ticket wholesale; and overseas non-life reinsurance underwriting business. In addition, it operates website, ue Planet. KNT-CT Holdings Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. KNT-CT Holdings Co., Ltd. operates as a subsidiary of Kintetsu Group Holdings Co.,Ltd.

Dkk-Toa Corporation manufactures and sells measuring instruments in Japan and internationally. It offers laboratory analytical instruments; analyzer for portable and boiler water, basic process, water and waste water treatment, and oil refineries; water quality analyzer and systems; ambient air / emission gas analyzers; and medical equipment. The company also engages in the maintenance and repairment of measuring equipment; and leasing of real estate properties.
