It also provides pit services, including oil change, tire change, installation of various accessories, bodywork and painting, vehicle inspection and maintenance, body and headlight coating, and interior cleaning services. In addition, the company engages in the purchase and sale of new and used cars, as well as the operation of Yakiniku-like franchisee restaurant. Buffalo Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1983 and is headquartered in Kawaguchi, Japan.

Ebrains, Inc. designs, develops, produces, and sells industrial electronics and industrial computers designed for information, communications, control, video, and measurement fields in Japan. The company offers CompactPCI standard products; advancedTCA backplane products; VME/VXI multibus II standard products comprising backplanes, DIN racks, universal boards, card extenders, unit power supplies, cabinets, and related devices; industrial system chassis with CPU boards and various I/O boards; and custom backplanes/custom racks/chassis. It also provides bus racks. Ebrains, Inc. was incorporated in 1973 and is headquartered in Hachioji, Japan.

Taisei Lamick Group Head Quarter & Innovation Co.,Ltd. develops and sells liquid and paste packaging systems in Japan. The company offers films, including XA-series and other products; and filling machines, such as DANGAN series products and filling peripheral equipment, as well as IoT cloud and product maintenance services. It also provides a filling training system. The company was formerly known as Taisei Lamick Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Taisei Lamick Group Head Quarter & Innovation Co.,Ltd. in April 2025. Taisei Lamick Group Head Quarter & Innovation Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1943 and is headquartered in Shiraoka, Japan.

Linkers Corporation engages in business matching and research in Japan. The company offers Linkers Sourcing, a service which searches for companies, universities, and experts to solve technological challenges in the manufacturing industry; and Linkers Marketing for opportunities for meetings with companies that show interest. It also provides Linkers for BANK/Business, a SaaS-based business matching systems; Linkers Research, which conducts research into various technical fields; and TechMesse Academy, a coordination of technology service promotion events. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

DAIKO XTECH, Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a systems integrator in Japan. The company engages in system consultancy; sale and maintenance of computers, and network equipment, including digital PBX; development and maintenance of software; sale of computer-related products; system engineering; network construction, which involves the construction of communication equipment; and construction of office facilities. It is also involved in insurance and mutual aid; building facility maintenance and management; labor dispatch, fee-paying employment placement, and hardware and software operation management; market development, sale, and post-implementation support for AppGuard security software; system development and outsourcing; SE dispatch; IT business support; information processing; computer business application development; and accounting system implementation consulting support activities. In addition, the company distributes information and communication equipment, such as information terminals, server and network equipment, electronic switching systems, and related peripherals and software. Further, it provides various software solutions for production management, PLM edition, distribution and service industry, mass retailers, special store edition, security, and infrastructure applications. The company was formerly known as Daiko Denshi Tsushin, Ltd. and changed its name to DAIKO XTECH, Ltd. in April 2025. DAIKO XTECH, Ltd. was incorporated in 1953 and is headquartered in Shinjuku, Japan.

R. C. Core Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, plans, designs, develops, and sells log houses and other unique natural home in Japan. The company offers construction contracts for log houses; develops and sells residential lands; and provides maintenance and renovation works. It is also involved in brokerage and sales of residential land and detached houses; non-residential construction contracting; and sales and management of vacation home timeshares. The company serves its products under the BESS brand. R. C. Core Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1985 and is headquartered in Shibuya, Japan.

PIXTA Inc. operates an online marketplace site of stock photos, illustrations, vectors, and footage in Japan. The company also operates an online marketplace that handles procurement to sales of digital materials, such as photos, illustrations, and videos on the internet. It serves designers, web directors, publishers, and other creative professionals. PIXTA Inc. was incorporated in 2005 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Tose Co., Ltd. plans, develops, and operates home video game software, and mobile content in Japan. It operates through Game Business and Other Business segments. The company also engages in the SI and non-game content businesses. It serves car and content providers. Tose Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1979 and is headquartered in Kyoto, Japan.

Sincere Co., LTD. manufacture and sells contact lenses in Japan. It operates through three segments: Contact Lens, Consulting, and System business. The company also engages in consulting on medical hair removal clinic management, as well as the design, development, sale, and maintenance of package systems for the reuse industry. In addition, it provides management support for medical institutions, medical related businesses, and elderly facility management. The company was incorporated in 2008 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Yamato International Inc. manufactures and sells textile products in Japan. The company offers casual wear for men and women, such as cut-and-sew knits, fabric shirts, flat knit sweaters, outerwear, bottoms, and other small items. It serves its products under the Crocodile, Crocodile ladies, Switch motion crocodile, Crocodile code, CITERA, Penfield, Aiman Marcus, and Lighting Bolt brands. The company also operates as a real estate rental business. The company was formerly known as Yamato Shirt Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Yamato International Inc. in March 1982. The company was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

LECIP Holdings Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, plans, designs, develops, manufactures, sells, and maintains transportation and industrial equipment in Japan, the United States, and internationally. The company offers fare boxes, liquid crystal displays, and LED destination indicators, as well as automated fare collection system, transit management system, and automotive lighting, including fluorescent lamps for trucks and passenger cars. It also provides chargers and power supply equipment, as well as power solutions, such as forklifts, automated guided vehicles, emergency uninterruptible power supplies, and others. In addition, the company offers product introduction support, maintenance, and maintenance services; mounting and assembling printed circuit boards. It serves buses, railways, automobiles, and industrial equipment industries. The company was formerly known as LECIP Corporation and changed its name to LECIP Holdings Corporation in October 2010. LECIP Holdings Corporation was founded in 1948 and is headquartered in Motosu, Japan.

Fuji Oozx Inc. engages in the manufacturing and sale of engine valves and other engine related components in Japan and internationally. The company offers engine valves, hollow valves, cotters/retainers, and valve seats and rotators. It also involved in processing of steel material, and sales of processed products; and tools and jigs, as well as engages in the sale, purchasing, repair, and lending of machine tools and equipment installment contract. The company was incorporated in 1951 and is headquartered in Kikugawa, Japan.

Soda Nikka Co., Ltd. engages in the trading of chemicals in Japan and internationally. The company offers inorganic chemicals used as dyes for natural fibers or sub materials in chemical fibers, for purification of drinking water or flavorings, and for glass and other materials for shelter; organic chemicals and petrochemical products used for detergent solutions or solvents and engineering plastics or electronic materials applications; paper pulp chemicals for wood chip digestion and bleaching during pulp manufacture, papermaking chemicals, and coating chemicals; environmental chemicals used in treatment of water and sewage, wastewater, waste gas, and contaminated soil; and industrial energy and fuel oil products. It also provides synthetic resins, film base materials and laminated plastic packaging products; electronic components, such as printed circuit boards, primary cells and rechargeable batteries, capacitors, connectors, and switches; environment-related equipment, construction work materials, industrial waste treatment products, and disaster preparedness tools and equipment; and new power solutions, including thermal insulation films and solar panels. Soda Nikka Co., Ltd. was founded in 1947 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

DVx Inc. engages in the research and development, design, purchasing/selling, import/export, repair, maintenance and inspection, and rental of medical devices and related peripheral devices in Japan. The company offers arrhythmia-related products, such as cardiac pacemakers, implantable cardioverter defibrillators, electrode catheters, and ablation catheters; ischemia-related products, that includes RAQUOS and resolution/resolution SD; and EPS Trainer, an intracardiac electrograms simulator; and wonder belt products. The company also engages in the design, manufacture, and purchasing/selling of medical computers; manufacture, purchasing/selling, repair, maintenance and inspection, rental, leasing, and import/export of medicines, quasi drugs, cosmetics, health foods, daily goods, nursing care products, and nursing care equipment; provision of internal and external medical and medical device-related general consulting and services, as well as medical publishing and market research services; and sale and purchase of second hand goods. The company was incorporated in 1986 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Estic Corporation designs, manufactures, and sells electric power tools, industrial robots, and automatic assembly lines in Europe, Africa, Asia, Oceania, and the Americas. The company's products include assembly tool, handheld and fixtured nutrunner, corded, cordless, and micro tools; automated machine; robotic tightening, scara and cartesian robot, and cobot solution; smart arms; tracer arm; press-fitting/caulking; and servo press. Estic Corporation was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Moriguchi, Japan.

Grandy House Corporation constructs and sells pre-built and custom-built homes in Japan. It is also involved in condominium sales, new and used home sales, new and used home renovation, construction material manufacturing and sales, and real estate leasing business. Grandy House Corporation was incorporated in 1991 and is headquartered in Utsunomiya, Japan.

SPK Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the trading of automotive spare parts and accessories, and industrial vehicle parts in Japan, Asia, Oceania, Central America, and South America. The company offers engines, drivetrain, brakes, suspension, electrical, and air conditioning parts; and oil chemical products. It also supplies automotive aftermarket parts and accessories; and machinery parts and components, including multifunction displays, integrated switches, dials, telematics equipment, and electrical components, such as lamps, resin parts, and functional parts to the manufacturers of construction vehicles, forklifts, and tractors. The company was formerly known as Daido Automobile Enterprising Co., Ltd. and changed its name to SPK Corporation in 1992. SPK Corporation was incorporated in 1917 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Mitani Corporation engages in the information systems, construction materials, energy, lifestyle-related business and development business in Japan and internationally. The company offers a range of software, including ERP, image-processing systems, and information infrastructure environments to manufacturing, construction, distribution, services, medical care and public administration clients; sells, builds, and supports computer-related systems for universities; and support investment and management of IT projects for academic institutions, including educational systems, portals, portfolios, and account management. It also provides information and communication technology related products; cement, soil improvement materials, ALC walls, spancrete, assembly manhole, fresh concrete, aggregates, extruded cement panels, hume pipes, as well as concrete-based secondary products and admixtures for concrete; and steel equipment, grating and other construction materials for roads, steel products, and other applications. In addition, the company offers gasoline, kerosene, crude oil, asphalt, liquid natural gas, coal, unsaturated polyester resins, gas oil, A/C heavy oil, lubricants, liquefied petroleum gas, general pressurized gases, plastics and secondary plastic products, kerosene heat pumps, Enefarm household fuel cells; paints, colorants, and fertilizers; chemical products; and LEDs and inverter fluorescent lights. Further, it provides construction equipment, store equipment, elevators, parking lots, solar power generation, hot-water pipes, and power generation products; and housing fixtures, water supply and drainage, HVAC, equipment for gas stations, office supplies, floor-panel heating, snow-melting facilities, real estate brokerage, leisure-related products, restaurants, lease services, and FRP-based swimming pools. Mitani Corporation was founded in 1914 and is headquartered in Fukui, Japan.

Rikei Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the system and network solutions, and electronic components and instrument businesses in Japan and internationally. It provides system solutions, including IT infrastructure, information security, and 3D CAD, CAM, and CAE solutions; IBM; Hewlett Packard enterprise solutions; entrance exam/curriculum and secure file collaboration systems; non-contact vital measurement and cloud SaaS solutions; IoT products; PCs; cloud authentication solution; software for power systems EMT simulations and calculating cable current carrying capacity; and AI products to private companies, universities, government offices, and research institutions. The company also offers information transmission and distribution network solutions to broadcasters, telecommunications, and local governments; and electronic components, instruments, and electronic materials for various sectors, including industrial equipment, automobile, medical, optical communication, and security. In addition, it engages in the sale and lease of aircraft and aircraft parts, and customer support; system development activities; and export, import, and sale of electronic products. The company was formerly known as Rikei Trading Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Rikei Corporation in June 1971. Rikei Corporation was incorporated in 1957 and is headquartered in Shinjuku, Japan.

MUGEN ESTATE Co.,Ltd. purchases and resells pre-owned properties in Japan. The company's property portfolio includes investment type properties, such as condominiums and office blocks and residential type properties, including detached houses for families. It engages in the development, leasing, and selling of real estate properties and interior and exterior construction business that includes renovate and refurbish stores, offices, office buildings, rental apartment complexes, and residential properties. The company was incorporated in 1990 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Nittoseiko Co.,Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells industrial fasteners and tools, industrial machinery and precision equipment, measurement and control equipment, and medical equipment in Japan, Asia, and internationally. It operates through Fastener; Industrial Machinery; Control; and Medical segments. The company offers screws for plastics and metal fastening, cold forged parts, anti-loosening screws, drive recesses, heads, self-clinching stud bolts, surface treatment products, waterproof screws, products for joining of dissimilar materials, and other metal parts; and screw driving motors, handheld type screw drivers, arm drivers, single and multi-spindle screw driving machines, screw driving robots, screw feeders, anti-contamination units, screw driving units for collaborative robots, caulking machines, assembly robots, and conveyors. It also provides flow meters, receivers/converters, micro-bubble washers, products for marine and batch applications, and geokartes. Nittoseiko Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1938 and is headquartered in Kyoto, Japan.

CAC Holdings Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides information technology services in Japan and internationally. It operates in two segments, IT and Overseas IT. The company constructs and operates information systems. It also offers systems development, integration, operation, management, consulting, and maintenance, as well as BPO/BTO services; develops and sells packaged software; and plans, develops, and sells production management systems. In addition, the company engages in the development of machine learning, artificial intelligence, and application, as well as new business; aquaculture using IoT and AI; development of mobile telecommunications products and communication systems, as well as registered telecommunications services; financial services and IT consulting; BPO services for waste management; help desk services; and software development. Further, it is involved in engineering and research and development services, outsourced product development, IT infrastructure management, managed services, and enterprise applications for banking, telecom, and manufacturing industries; and sale and maintenance of software products. The company was formerly known as CAC Corporation and changed its name to CAC Holdings Corporation in April 2014. CAC Holdings Corporation was founded in 1966 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

STUDIO ATAO Co., Ltd. engages in planning and sale of bags and wallets in Japan. The company also manages stores and internet stores; and plans and sells character products. It offers its products the ATAO, ILEMER, IANNE, StrawberryMe, and Atelier Atao brands. STUDIO ATAO Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 2005 and is headquartered in Kobe, Japan.

NSW Inc. operates as an enterprise, services, embedded, and device solutions provider in Japan. It provides business solutions, such as consulting, system design, development, maintenance, and operation solutions for manufacturing, distribution, finance, insurance, and government sectors. The company also offers IT services, including servers and networks designing; secure environment development; and operating and monitoring systems, as well as IoT & AI services. In addition, it is involved in the embedded system development business, which include development of applications, middleware, and drivers for automotive and industrial sectors; and edge device solutions. Further, it develops LSI and FPGA for image processing and communication control fields. The company was formerly known as Nippon Systemware Co., Ltd. and changed its name to NSW Inc. in June 2022. NSW Inc. was incorporated in 1966 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Tachikawa Corporation designs, manufactures, markets, sells, and installs various window covering products, centered around blinds and room partitions primarily in Japan. The company offers vertical and venetian blinds, roller blinds, roman shades, transitional blinds, pleated blinds, wood blinds, partition walls, accordion doors, curtain rails, and other window coverings and interior finishing products for home, office, and public buildings. It is also involved in the manufacture and sale of parking systems, geared motors, motorized panel louvers, and movable partitions, as well as offers residential, office, and public facility solutions. The company was formerly known as Tachikawa Manufacturing Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Tachikawa Corporation in October 1947. The company was founded in 1938 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Zoa Corporation engages in the retail sale of personal computers, peripherals, and motorcycle accessories. It provides printers, tablets, parts for assembling personal computers, monitors, hard disks, network devices, external storage devices, internal storage devices, CPU, memory, motherboard, graphic board, etc., as well as business software, game software, PC accessories, ink, papers, media, etc. The company also offers motorcycle related products, such as motorcycle parts, helmets, gloves, clothing, car accessories, etc.; and support services. In addition, it engages in buying, selling, and leasing real estate, such as land and buildings. The company was founded in 1981 and is headquartered in Numazu, Japan.

GameWith Inc. engages in media and esports and entertainment businesses in Japan. The company operates GameWith, which provides information on strategies in the game, articles introducing new games, and other contents. It also manages creators, such as game play video streamers, as well as e-sports teams. In addition, the company provides support and services, including video production and editing support, acquiring corporate tie-up projects, planning collaborative events, and reaching users. Further, it is involved in NFT business and fiber optic line businesses. GameWith Inc. was incorporated in 2013 and is based in Minato, Japan.

Ekitan & Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides information services using the internet in Japan. It operates through Mobility Support Business, Advertising Distribution Platform Business, and M&A/Incubation Business segments. The company offers consumer services comprising transfer/timetable/operation information search services on PC and smartphone websites and apps; Ekitan PICKS, which contains information on station-based stores and services; and member special services. It also provides corporate services consisting of transit/route planning API, which is a search function used for route search/fare and fee search; transportation expense settlement/commuting expense calculation services, which offers route search, fare search, and commuter pass search functions used in the cloud; domestic ticketless business trip arrangement services, which allows for the arrangement of discounted tickets through corporate contracts; data sales/generation services on stations, lines, and trains used in transit guides; MaaS solutions that solve regional mobility, transportation, and tourism issues; and advertising/alliance services on Ekitan's consumer website and app. In addition, the company offers paid subscription services; transit information ASP for mobile carriers, railway companies, and map companies; travel guidebook systems; and system-related operations, including development and maintenance, as well as sells marketing ASPs. Further, it is involved in the planning, design, development, production, and sale of computer-based software and websites, etc.; research and consulting services related to information provision and information processing; and temporary worker dispatch activities. Ekitan & Co., Ltd. was founded in 1997 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Wavelock Holdings Co., Ltd., through its subsidiaries, provides interior, material, and technology solutions in Japan and internationally. The company manufactures and sells plastic sheets/films, synthetic fiber net products, mesh, other processed products, and related products; and processes, manufactures, markets, sells, and consults synthetic resins and various materials. It also processes, markets, and insect repellent products and various other materials; imports and sells synthetic resin or miscellaneous materials; and markets various electronic materials, plastic products, etc. In addition, the company engages in geothermal heat related equipment construction works; civil engineering works; pipe work and pavement works; water supply facilities construction works; and electrical construction works. It also offers petrochemical-derived resin products for construction, housing, and agricultural fields. The company was formerly known as Japan Wavelock co.,Ltd. and chaged its name to Wavelock Holdings Co., Ltd. in April 2005. The company was incorporated in 1964 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon RAD Inc. engages in enterprise solution and IoT integration businesses in Japan. The company offers IoT platforms; IoT real-time visualization; and equipment maintenance management system. It also provides SeekFak which allows anyone to easily detect deepfake images and video; kinterp, a business system that adds ERP elements to the business application development platform kintone; kinterp DB, which provides Automatic data synchronization; kintone, a business application creation platform and cloud database service; Counting Master Atabor 5, used to streamline the distribution, collection, and aggregation of excel work; Attack Board, an on-premise data integration platform; ETLanTIS, an application development tool with data integration functionality; Tortel for My Number Card; Tortel a/Tortel; warehouse management system; POS-linked customer management system; and Hadoop, an open source distributed processing system that performs uniform batch processing on big data. In addition, the company offers medical solutions, including outpatient services and other/research support solutions; and BI, picture, and security solutions. Nippon RAD Inc. was founded in 1971 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Asian Star Co. engages in the planning, development and sale, holding and leasing, management, and brokerage of real estate properties in Japan. The company develops and sells detached and resort houses, manages and operates rental apartments, as well as provides real estate investment advisory services. It also offers non-life insurance agency services. The company was formerly known as Yoko Toshikaihatsu Co. and changed its name to Asian Star Co. in April 2015. Asian Star Co. was founded in 1979 and is based in Yokohama, Japan.

Fund Creation Group Company Limited engages in the asset management and investment banking businesses in Japan. The company invests in the fields of real estate and solar power businesses, as well as in the securities. It is also involved in the vehicle leasing; and real estate crowdfunding business. In addition, the company offers financial product intermediation services. Fund Creation Group Company Limited was founded in 2002 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Hoshi Iryo-Sanki Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells pharmaceutical medical oxygen and nitrous oxides in Japan. It operates through the five segments: Medical Gas Related Business; Home Medical Care Related Business; Medical Gas Equipment Construction Related Business; Nursing Care and Welfare Related Business; and Nursing Care Facility-Related Business. The Medical Gas Related Business segment sells medical oxygen gas, anesthetic gas, and other medical gases. The Home Medical Care Related Business segment rents and sells oxygen concentrators for home oxygen therapy, and continuous positive airway and sales. The Medical Gas Equipment Construction Related Business segment designs and constructs medical gas piping works, as well as maintains and inspects medical gas piping equipment. The Nursing Care and Welfare Related Business segment offers rental and sale of care and welfare-related products and equipment, home renovation, visiting nursing, and home care support services. The Nursing Care Facility-Related Business segment operates the paid nursing homes and day care facilities, and other related operations. The company was founded in 1960 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Optoelectronics Co., Ltd. develops, manufactures, and sells automatic identification devices in Japan, the United States the America, Europe, Asia, and internationally. It offers 2D imager and 1D laser/CCD module engines, fixed and handheld scanners, desktop/built-in scanners, data collectors, and handheld terminals. Optoelectronics Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1976 and is headquartered in Warabi, Japan.

Daiichi Jitsugyo Co., Ltd., engages in the import, export, and sale of plant and machinery equipment in Japan. The company offers natural resources development, petroleum, chemicals, pulp manufacturing equipment, decarbonization and renewable energy solutions, digital transformation, and engineering services. It also provides lithium-ion battery and fuel cell, as well as research and development on next-generation batteries; and recycling services. In addition, the company offers industrial machinery, including home appliances/office automation, food packaging, medical and housing equipment, distribution materials, and strawberry cultivation seedlings; and electronic products, such as surface mount technology, logistics automation solution, and semiconductors and electronic components, as well as assembly lines, automated machines, and custom-built equipment. Further, it provides interior and exterior parts, powertrain, car electronics/adas products; technology and equipment development, marketing, and consulting services; tow tractors, aircraft de-icers, container loaders, and passenger stairs; airport rescue and fire fighting, airport snow removal vehicles, runway friction testers, medical equipment transport vehicles, glycol recovery vehicles, and automatic explosive detection systems for airport companies; Disaster Preparedness/defense; and maintenance, repair, and spare parts supply. Daiichi Jitsugyo Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1948 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

UNIRITA Inc. provides software and solutions for information technology (IT) systems operation in Japan and internationally. The company offers auttit, a SaaS-based job management cloud service; DigiSheet and The Staff-V, which are personnel management cloud services; and Waha!Transformer. Its products LMIS, a lifecycle management product for IT services; A-AUTO, an automatic job scheduling and monitoring system; Sky-Eye Tribune, a monitoring system that includes resource management, network management, server management, and desktop management; TIA Report, a total information analysis report system; BSP-RM, a multiplatform report management system; Exentry, a data entry system; Be.Learning, an e-learning system for IT systems operations management; A-SPOOL, a report system management; and A-LOG, a job log and system log management system. The company also provides related consulting and technical support services. It serves corporate clients and businesses. UNIRITA Inc. was incorporated in 1982 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Tokyo Kisen Co.,Ltd. engages in the tugboat business in Japan and internationally. It operates through Tugboat, Passenger Ship, and Store and Restaurant segments. The company provides harbor tug services; escort tug services; emergency support and disaster prevention services; domestic and international tugboat network; offshore wind power access vessel business; and logistics support to maritime industry, as well as provides car ferry services. It is also involved in towing business, pilot boat operation business, ship chartering business, and related businesses. The company was incorporated in 1947 and is headquartered in Yokohama-shi, Japan.

Yokogawa Bridge Holdings Corp. engages in the design, fabrication, and construction of steel structures in Japan and internationally. It operates through Bridge Business, Engineering Business, Precision Equipment Business and Real Estate Business segments. The company designs, fabricates, and erects steel bridge projects; and maintains, repairs, and retrofits existing structures. It is also involved in the supply of structural steel buildings for factories, storehouses, shops, and sports facilities; fabrication of port and offshore structures; and construction of high-rise buildings and dome-type sports stadiums, as well as provision of machinery for the infrastructure sector, such as water gate, shiplift facility, and sewage treatment. In addition, the company supplies metal frames for use in the production of semiconductors and flat panel displays; and provides software for the total design system for steel bridges and simulation systems for the erection of steel structures, etc. Yokogawa Bridge Holdings Corp. was founded in 1907 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

F.C.C. Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells clutches for automobiles, motorcycles, and general-purpose machinery in Japan and internationally. It offers clutches, such as wet-type multi-disc, wet-type multi-disc assisted and slipper, wet-type and dry centrifugal, and racing clutches, as well as pulleys for belt converters for use in motorcycles, road sports bikes, scooters, ATVs, and racing vehicles. The company provides automobiles clutches, including automatic transmission, HEV, CVT, torque converter lockup, manual transmission, and 4WD vehicle clutches. In addition, it offers general-purpose clutches, such as FCCTRAC, a cam-type torque-sensitive limited slip differential for four-wheel ATVs, and hydraulic static transmissions for snow throwers and lawn mowers. The company was formerly known as Fuji Chemical Co., Ltd. and changed its name to F.C.C. Co., Ltd. in July 1984. F.C.C. Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1939 and is headquartered in Hamamatsu, Japan.

monoAI technology Co.,Ltd. provides metaverse, and XR event and peripheral services in Japan. It offers XR CLOUD that allows for easy hosting of events in virtual spaces; monoXR; mono QA; monobit engine; and AI implementation support services. The company was incorporated in 2013 and is headquartered in Kobe, Japan.

Nihon Kagaku Sangyo Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of industrial chemicals and building materials in Japan and internationally. It offers various inorganic metal products, including copper, tin, nickel, cobalt, bismuth, chromium, manganese, antimony, zinc, iron, and lithium compounds; organic metal compounds, such as metal acetylacetonate, metallic soaps, PUCAT, and PA series products; surface treatment agents, including anodized aluminum treatment, electro plating, electro plating brightener/additives, electroless plating, etchant agent, and rust prevention; and other compounds. The company's chemical products are used in various industries, such as optical/precision instruments, electronics, ceramics, petrochemical catalysis, healthcare, and battery materials. It also develops fireproof, waterproof, and ventilation building materials; and heat exchangers, as well as offers stainless-steel handrails and mailboxes; fabricated metal products for industrial use; and materials used in eaves, as well as pipe space/blindfold covers and exterior products. The company was incorporated in 1924 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Rasa Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, trades in and supplies various products in Japan, Singapore, China, Thailand, Asia, and internationally. It operates through six segments: Resources and Metallic Materials, Industrial and Construction Machineries, Environmental Equipment, Chemical, Industrial Plants and Equipment, and Real Estate. The company imports, exports, and sells mineral sand products, including zircon and rutile sand, ilmenite, and others; industrial materials, such as phosphoric, anhydrous phosphoric, and phosphorous acid, second phosphorus finished goods, malp, aluminum hydroxide, polychlorinated aluminum, aluminum sulfate, metallic manganese, metallic silicon and nickel, talc, China clay, silica, boron carbide, electrolytic manganese, and others; and refractory raw materials comprising calcined, low soda, spherical, and other fused alumina, zirconium and lithium compounds, fused spinel, spherical and fused silica power, graphite, black and green silicon carbide, B4C, and others. It also offers industrial machineries, which include pumps, valves, mixers, crushers, strainers, cyclons, pressure washers, rubber joints, and others; construction equipment, such as pipe jacking machines, handheld gas-powered breakers, pipe cutting machines, concrete pumps/mixers, mini crawler cranes, and others; and environmental equipment comprising RASA slag granulation system and PARA-ECO-recycling system. In addition, the company provides plastic synthetic resins and resin additives; sheets, resin films, tapes, and metal foils; extruded and injection-molded materials; oil and fat, and other chemical products. Further, it is involved in the design, installation, and maintenance services for equipment used in oil refineries, petrochemical plants, and other production facilities; and revitalization and leasing of real estate properties. The company was incorporated in 1939 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Stanley Electric Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the manufacture, sale, and import/export of automotive and other light bulbs in Japan and internationally. The company operates in automotive equipment products, component products, and electronic application products segments. It offers headlamp and turn signal lamp; rear combination lamp; interior; automotive light bulbs and accessories; visible light source; UV Light Source; Infrared Light Source; sensor; MEMS mirror; display; lighting fixtures; UV disinfection products; operation panels; backlight units; and medical products. Stanley Electric Co., Ltd. was founded in 1920 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Phil Company, Inc. provides space solutions in Japan. It engages in the construction of Phil Park, an aerial retail store; and Premium Garage House, a rental housing with attached garage. Phil Company, Inc. was incorporated in 2005 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Keyware Solutions Inc. develops and sells information systems for the support of social infrastructure in Japan. It offers medical service systems to various medical services divisions, such as examination, nutrition, medical professions, and clinic management analysis systems; transportation operations, includes fare collection, train station revenue administration, settlement with other train lines, and IC cards; social infrastructure solutions; public traffic systems, such as new traffic management and train operation information network systems; passengers-related information systems, including train approaching status displays and destination guide displays, as well as train seat reservation system; and mass media related solutions. The company also provides communication data for fixed phones and mobile phones, packet communication data, customer management, rate management, billing administration, collection administration, and linkage and backbone systems; and air traffic and monitoring control management systems, as well as mobile telecommunications. In addition, it offers various solutions, including applications for ERP, patent administration, medical services, and IP telephony; and support services, including creation, implementation, operation, maintenance, and training services; system integration services, as well as sells hardware, systems, package software products, and related supplies. It offers its products and services for use in financial services, distribution and logistics, manufacturing, telecommunications, transportation, media, government agencies, medical services, network administration, system monitoring, transmission control, and interface control fields, as well as for service providers. The company was formerly known as Nippon Electronics Development Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Keyware Solutions Inc. in January 2001. Keyware Solutions Inc. was founded in 1965 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

JTEC Corporation engages in the technical staff intellectual property leasing business for engineers in Japan and internationally. It specializes in the dispatch of engineers to the development and design departments of manufacturing companies. It also operates in human resource businesses, nursing care businesses, event businesses, and posting businesses. The group focuses on recruitment, human resource development, and sales as the main pillars of sustainable business development. It provides services in areas requiring advanced skills, such as mechanical design, electrical and electronic design, and software, and is involved in research and development and design in four fields, including architectural design. The company also offers reskilling and re-career development services, technical education, and comprehensive career support, including compliance, ethics, and mental health. It serves manufacturing companies and other business clients in Japan. The company was incorporated in 1996 and is based in Chuo, Japan.

TV Asahi Holdings Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the television (TV) broadcasting business in Japan and internationally. It operates through the TV Broadcasting Business, Internet Business, Shopping Business, and Other Businesses segments. The TV Broadcasting Business segment is involved in the sale of commercial time slots, as well as the operation of broadcasting satellite and commercial satellite businesses. Its Internet Business segment provides subscription-video-on-demand services and advertising video on demand service that comprises TVer and TV Asahi catch-up service; and operation of internet video ad distribution platform. The Shopping Business segment sells goods through television shopping programs, e-commerce, and retail. Its Other Businesses segment engages in music publication, special events production, sales of DVDs and videos, investments in motion pictures, property rental, and the sales and lease of broadcasting equipment. TV Asahi Holdings Corporation was incorporated in 1957 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

CNS Co.,Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides system engineering services in Japan. It offers system integration services, including cloud environment construction, infrastructure environment construction, application development, modernization, data utilization/analysis, SAS introduction support, and ServiceNow implementation support; U-Way oracle cloud infrastructure, U-Way oracle cloud VMware solution, and U-Way Lite OCI base model services; and DX, technology, and business consulting services. The company also provides system infrastructure, big data analysis, and digital innovation promotion services; and DX literacy education and human resource development accompaniment support services. CNS Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1985 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Starts Publishing Corporation engages in the book content and media solutions business in Japan. The company publishes magazines containing media, media data, and advertising media materials under the OZmagazine, metromin, AELDE, OZmall, and OzReporters brands. It also provides OZ premium reservation services, which allow online booking for hotels, restaurants, and beauty salons; and books and e-book labels, comics and digital comic labels, and film and TV adaptation rights services. In addition, the company operates websites, such as Wild Strawberries, Berry's Café, and Novema! Further, it produces internal communication and sales support tools, including brochures, posters, websites, company histories, videos, and comics. Starts Publishing Corporation was incorporated in 1983 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Hatsuho Shouji Co.,Ltd. trades in steel and construction materials in Japan. The company offers interior building materials, such as steel ceiling framing, steel wall base, gypsum board, glass wool, and residential insulation materials, as well as ceiling, non-combustible board, rock wool sound-absorbing panels, bis, suspension bolts, ceiling access and underfloor inspection hatch, trim molding and glass container, bathroom ceiling panel joiner, PVC ceiling trim/magar, PVC angle joiner, wallpaper base corner, aluminum ceiling trim and angles, aluminum joiner, ceiling looper, glue, deck hanging bracket, spandrel, soundproofing sheet and partitions, soundproofing panels, rock wool, OA floors, steel flooring material, insulation panel, lead board, royal stud, helmet, hangall, picture rail, welding machine, LVL, nonkiriko, and machine tools. It also provides exterior products, such as carports, sheds, fences, and stone materials to house builders and exterior construction companies; and water-based glass shatterproof coating, LED, non-flammable lightweight ceiling, seismic isolation and control system, and flood control solutions. In addition, the company offers housing and environment-related products, including colored steel sheets, solar power roofing, ALC metal fittings and auxiliary materials, and ceramic building material fittings and auxiliary materials, as well as architectural hardware, welded wire mesh, and iron wire products. Further, it provides metal siding, rain gutters, sandwich panels, sheet metal tools, galvalume steel roofing and exterior wall molding; ceiling lighting fixtures, industrial pressure fan, battery storage, drill screws, polycarbonate corrugated sheet, sheet metal mounting hardware, metal joiner, rooftop ventilation fan, cutting wheel; nails, screws, breathable sheet, anchor bolts, bicycle rack, nameplate, binding wire, grating; and annealed wire products. Hatsuho Shouji Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1946 and is headquartered in Nagoya, Japan.

CAVE Interactive CO.,LTD. engages in developing and operating mobile online games in Japan. The company also offers support and management for video streamers, including internet advertising. CAVE Interactive CO.,LTD. was incorporated in 1994 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Signal Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the manufacture and sale of railway and road traffic signal solutions. The company operates through the Transportation Infrastructure Business and ICT Solution Business segments. It offers railway signal safety and road traffic systems, station service automation systems, security solutions, parking lot systems, robotics solutions, platform safety systems, 3D laser ranging image sensors, and ground penetrating radar solutions. The company also offers maintenance services for railway signal safety equipment, AFC and parking equipment, and road traffic safety systems. Nippon Signal Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1928 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

The Nihon Seima Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells food products, industrial materials, and floor mats in Japan. The company provides food products, such as spaghetti, soup, curry, pasta sauce, and retort products. It offers jute and kenaf products, hemp bags, sandbags, binder string, linen thread, wire interposed yarn, carpet backing, hemp rope, rope, heat-retaining sheet for composite materials, Rivet bag, Hessian bag, Tamaito thread, and other PE/PP bags, as well as industrial materials such as plastic bags; and heavy packaging paper bag, kraft paper bags, easy-open bag, sewing bag, double-stitched bags, one-side stitched bag, bag for rice and wheat, rice inspection bag, PV bag, BAX bag, PE products, PP products, flexible container, corrugated cardboard products, civil engineering materials, stretch film, bag-making materials, and other transport materials. In addition, the company provides car mats, including tufted carpet, Hook Drag, Rubber mats, resin mats, etc. It offers its food products under the Volcano brand. The company was formerly known as Nakagoe Boshoku Co., Ltd. and changed its name to The Nihon Seima Co.,Ltd. in May 1959. The Nihon Seima Co.,Ltd. was founded in 1918 and is headquartered in Tonami, Japan.

Hisaka Works, Ltd. manufactures and sells industrial machinery in Japan and internationally. It operates through Heat Exchanger, Process Engineering, and Valve segments. The Heat Exchanger segment offers multi gap plates, semi-welded plates, fin tubes, double-lined gasket plates, plate condensers, corrugated plate patterns, dual wall plates, brazed plate heat exchangers, and sea water solutions. This segment's products are used in various applications, such as general manufacturing, chemicals, pulp and paper, pure water, cooking oil, steelmaking, petroleum refining, ships, soda, electricity, beer, and sulfuric acid. The Process Engineering segment provides food machinery, such as sterilizers, evaporator-concentrator systems, factory automation systems, and testing machine and other equipment; pharmaceutical purposes sterilizers, pharmaceutical liquid continuous sterilizers, evaporation concentrator systems, pure steam generators, plant engineering, and development test laboratory; and dyeing and finishing machinery consisting of liquid dyeing, multi-purpose air jet processing, dyeing processing, beam-dyeing, and cheese dyeing/drying machines, as well as options for dyeing equipment. The Valve segment offers various valves, such as 2-way ball, 3-way ball, 4-way ball, and diaphragm valves. The Other segment engages in the solar power generation business. The company was formerly known as MITSUISHI Ind. and changed its name to Hisaka Works, Ltd. in August 1951. Hisaka Works, Ltd. was incorporated in 1942 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Noile-Immune Biotech Inc. researches and develops CAR-T cells for novel cancer immunotherapies. The company's product pipeline includes, NIB101, a target antigen for solid tumors; NIB102, a next-generation CAR-T cell therapy; NIB103, autologous CAR-T cell therapy for mesothelin (MSLN); NIB104, a PRIME technology against target antigens in solid tumors; ADAP01; AUTL01; and CHUG01. Noile-Immune Biotech Inc. was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

Nichirin Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells automotive and housing hoses, and related parts in Japan and internationally. The company offers braking, hydraulic and pneumatic hoses for automobiles and motorcycles; rubber and resin hoses; and piping for heat management and hot and cold-water supply services. It also provides automotive hoses, including those for steering, braking, and air conditioning systems, as well as hose assemblies and piping for large trucks and buses. It also manufactures and sells products for non-automotive sectors, such as home appliances and housing equipment, and components for electric vehicles. Nichirin Co., Ltd. was founded in 1914 and is headquartered in Kobe, Japan.

Asahi Intelligence Service Co., Ltd. provides information processing services in Japan. The company is also involved in the construction, operation, and management of LAN/WAN products, and various Windows/UNIX servers; and provision of system integration and system operation management. In addition, it offers software maintenance, operation construction support, and system operation improvement; and maintains, operates, and manages host computer systems; office automation support; and installs software and provides information services, such as help desk. Asahi Intelligence Service Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1962 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

TORQ Inc. engages in the sale of fastening products in Japan. The company offers various inserts, concrete spacers, PC nuts, coupler and washer, anchor plates, hardware for manhole, stick inserts, flange hardware, connecting plates, and lifting jig; and material handling and transportation equipment, civil engineering and construction equipment, welding/fusing instruments, work/measuring tools, safety and disaster prevention goods, hydraulic equipment, air and electric tools, electrical installation and plumbing fixture, and cutting/grinding tools. It also provides handled products; zinc hat series; anchor birds; new lock anchor; new lock nut; return buckle, multi eye bolt and multi eye bolt hybrid yokobee; round lock bolt/nut; line cap; nord-lock washers; unbrako; bumax; and zennehji marker. The company was formerly known as Kobayashi Metals Limited and changed its name to TORQ Inc. in April 2020. TORQ Inc. was founded in 1926 and is based in Osaka, Japan.

Sanyo Shokai Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of men's and women's clothes and accessories in Japan. It sells its products through department stores, specialty stores, and directly managed stores. The company was founded in 1942 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

AGEHA Inc. provides general branding support services in Japan. It also offers corporate, inner, sustainability, purpose, outer, product and service, and recruitment branding support services, as well as digital marketing support services. AGEHA Inc. was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

Shindengen Electric Manufacturing Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells semiconductors, electrical equipment, and power supply products in Japan. It operates in three segments: Device Business, Electrical equipment business, and Energy systems business. The company offers DC quick charger, AC charger, wireless charging system, engine and motor control, battery power supply, damper, power steering, and lighting control products; and energy storage systems ad inverters, inverter units, PV inverters, monitoring systems, and other products. It also provides industrial equipment, LED lighting, AC/DC power supplies, internet infrastructure, data centers, and telecommunication power supplies, semiconductor power. It serves the mobility, energy, industrial equipment, telecommunication, and home appliance markets. Shindengen Electric Manufacturing Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1949 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.
Dairei Co.,Ltd. plans, develops, and sells frozen food products for professional use in Japan. The company was incorporated in 1972 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Trenders, Inc. engages in the marketing business in Japan. It is also involved in and investment business. The company provides promotion and PR support services. It also operates e-commerce malls specializing in clinic-exclusive products. In addition, The is also involved in media management; agency sale of imported brands; production and sale of e-books and webtoons; and planning, manufacturing, and sale of clinic cosmetics and health foods. Trenders, Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Yutaka Foods Corporation manufactures and sells food products in Japan. It offers granular food, liquid seasoning food, such as soy sauces, dashi, miso processed products, as well as provides dashi and miso processed products. The company also provides powder mixing, granule products, solid products, and tableting. In addition, it offers noodles comprising chilled /instant noodles and other products. The company was founded in 1919 and is based in Chita, Japan.

Tiemco Ltd. plans, develops, manufactures, imports, exports, and sells fishing and outdoor products in Japan. The company offers fly hooks, rods, and reels; fly lines comprising floating, thinking, thinking tips, shooting tapers and lines, double hands, saltwater, and backing lines; leader tippets; accessories consisting of fly boxes, line maintenance, fried dressings, indicators, and line cutters; fly tying tools, such as tying vises and tools; fly tying materials, including hackle feathers, hair, dubbing, synthetic, bead weight eyes, thread wires, cement, glue, and wax; finished fried food; polarized sunglasses; apparel and gear; fishing gear; and bags and accessories. It also provides bass, trout, trolling, spinning, and baitcasting rods; bass lures, such as big baits, crankbaits, vibration, minnow shad, topwater, wire baits, frog, rubber and metal jigs, prop baits, and soft lures; trout lures, which include minnow, spoon, and jigs; hard, soft, and ayu lures; snakehead fish; and small fish fishing products. In addition, the company offers men's and women's shirts, jackets, pants and tights, vests, rain gear, and cut and sewn tops; hats and caps, wet wading, fishing accessories, shoes, fishing jackets, wading shoes, insect-repellant clothing, fishing vests, fishing rain gear, and fishing bags. It sells its products through its stores and online under the PDL, TMC, AMPKA, Oracle, Orvis, Cascade, Garki, Gear Aid, Scientific Anglers, Site Master, Sunrise Fly Tying Tools, Semperfly, Tiemco, TIEMCO LURES, Benecchi, Foxfire, Fenwick, Flying Fisherman, Hareline Dubbin, Metz, Richard Whitley, Loop, Renzetti, Ross Reels, UNI, and Euflex brands. Tiemco Ltd. was incorporated in 1969 and is headquartered in Sumida, Japan.

Yokota Manufacturing Co., Ltd., provide fluid control solutions primarily for Water in Japan and internationally. The company develops, manufactures, and sells pumps, valves, devices, special alloys, and parts. The company offers self-priming centrifugal, enhanced self-priming, de-foaming/degassing, multistage and enhanced self-priming, turbine, process, centrifugal, multistage centrifugal, double suction volute, and mixed-flow volute pumps. It also provides non-water hammer check valves; wafer-type check valves; constant flow regulator valves; float-type regulator valves; and pilot-type regulator valves, including constant pressure, constant flow rate, liquid level-1 regulator, liquid level-2 regulator, safety, pressure relief, pressure sustaining, differential pressure regulator, and emergency shutoff valves. In addition, the company offers liquid-air switch valves and automatic-cleaning strainers; and special and seawater resistant stainless steel, casting parts, and non-external-flushing mechanical seal products. Further, the company provides installation and piping services. The company's products are used in ultra-pure water production, agricultural irrigation, civil engineering, construction, electric power generation, city water, sewage, chemical processing, plant equipment, food, medicine, aquariums, fisheries, and hot springs applications. It serves government and municipal offices, and other companies. The company was formerly known as Yokota Pump Manufacturing Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Yokota Manufacturing Co., Ltd. in 1959. Yokota Manufacturing Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1953 and is headquartered in Hiroshima, Japan.

Wealth Management, Inc. primarily operates hotels. It also provides digital real estate securitization and asset management services. The company was formerly known as Dreamvisor Holdings, Inc. and changed its name to Wealth Management, Inc. in October 2014. The company was incorporated in 1999 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Extreme Co.,Ltd. engages in the digital human resource, contract development, and content property businesses in Japan. The company offers customer-resident smartphone application, IT, and WEB software development services; and smartphone apps, cloud platform contract development services. It also provides various games, digital content, web systems, and license services. Extreme Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 2005 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Maezawa Industries, Inc. engages in the manufacture, sale, and installation of equipment and apparatus for water supply and sewage systems in Japan and internationally. The company offers valve products, such as gate, control, butterfly, check, eccentric, air, hydrant, maintenance, emergency shutoff, and sluice gate and weir valves. It also provides water treatment, including water purification, wastewater treatment, industrial environment, and water supply. The company was founded in 1937 and is headquartered in Kawaguchi, Japan

IwaiCosmo Holdings, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides financial services using information technology in Japan. It also offers asset management services to individual investors. The company was formerly known as Iwai Securities Co., Ltd. and changed its name to IwaiCosmo Holdings, Inc. in July 2010. IwaiCosmo Holdings, Inc. was incorporated in 1944 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

Gendai Agency Inc. engages in planning and production of advertising and promotion services in Japan. The company is involved in planning and production of advertisements using internet media and mass media, such as television, radio, newspapers, and magazines; newspaper insert advertising planning and production; planning and production of direct mail, posters for interior and exterior store decoration, POP and other printed materials, novelties, etc. It also engages in video and web contents production; advertisement planning and media slot sales; planning and production of ads, etc.; operation of customer referral support media; leasing of real estate and office brokerage; and research and development for new service and new business model activities, as well as planning and management of store events. The company was founded in 1994 and is headquartered in Shinjuku, Japan.

THine Electronics, Inc., a fabless semiconductor company, engages in the planning, designing, and sale of mixed-signal large scale integrations (LSIs) in Japan and internationally. It operates through LSI Business and AIOT Business segments. The company offers V-by-One HS that supports the higher resolutions required by flat panel displays; V-by-One for solving skew problems, EMI emission, and space of printed circuit boards of a system; low-voltage differential signaling (LVDS) products; V-by-One HS plus, an advanced high-speed serial interface standard for higher resolution comprising 4K/8K and higher refresh rate over 120Hz on high-speed video inter connect; IOHA:B transceivers; and USB products. It also provides power modules, RGB and backlight LED drivers, image signal processors and camera development kits, video ADCs, camera systems and video extension solutions, and fever detection and facial recognition systems. The company's products are used in CameraLink and USB/UVC cameras; video teleconference system, such as digital signages 4K sports/action cameras; security and body cameras; video conference systems and printers; gaming monitors and machines; smart city/commerce applications; medical monitors and endoscopes; and drive recorders and electron mirrors applications. THine Electronics, Inc. was founded in 1991 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Regal Corporation manufactures, sells, and repairs various shoes for men and women. The company serves its products under the REGAL, SHETLANDFOX, KENFORD, ESSENTIAL PRODUCTS, BEAUFIT, camelot, R., REDWOOD RIVER, Clarks, POLO RALPH LAUREN, LAUREN RALPH LAUREN, CLAE, Himiko, NICAL, and other brands. It sells its products through stores and online stores. The company was formerly known as Nippon Shoe Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Regal Corporation in October 1990. Regal Corporation was incorporated in 1902 and is headquartered in Urayasu, Japan.

Kawakami Paint Mfg.Co.,Ltd. manufactures and sells various paints in Japan. The company offers paint products, such as heat-shielding paint, ultra-low temperature powder, neo series, and water-based nado ace; and roof, floor, exterior building, and interior building paints. It also provides anti-rust paint; water-based paint; and fire-retardant paint. The company was founded in 1901 and is headquartered in Amagasaki, Japan.

CellSource Co., Ltd. engages in the regenerative medicine-related businesses in Japan. The company provides processing services to extract adipose-derived stem cells from patients' adipose tissues, and to prepare platelet-rich plasma from patients' blood; and offers support services for medical facilities providing regenerative medicine therapy. It is also involved in the sale of human stem cell-derived cosmetic ingredients; and Signalift series of anti-aging products, including a serum. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in Shibuya, Japan.

Nagaileben Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells medical clothing in Japan. The company engages in sale, import, and export of various white clothing and hospital shoes and socks. It also offers hospital, clinic, surgical, and nursing facility clothing products, as well as surgical linen. The company was founded in 1915 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

TOA Corporation manufactures and sells broadcasting, communications, and transmission equipment in Japan. The company offers public address / voice evacuation systems, commercial audio systems, professional sound systems, intercom systems, wireless systems, and conference systems. It also provides security camera systems, such as coaxial cable camera system and network camera systems; loudspeakers, PA systems, and wireless systems; remote broadcasting equipment and audio transmission equipment; railway vehicle-related products; audio and video-related products; and railroad vehicle-related products. In addition, the company is involved in the design, rental, and installation of audio and visual equipment, electrical, and electronic devices; and engineering and construction of audio and video-related products. Further, it plans, produces, and sells audio and video software, as well as provides telecommunications services. Additionally, the company engages in the rental and management of halls and studios; and plans and manages musical events. TOA Corporation was founded in 1934 and is headquartered in Kobe, Japan.

Chugai Ro Co., Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, engages in the development of technologies in the thermal technology field in Japan and internationally. It offers steel and nonferrous-metal heating furnaces, and heat treat furnaces; metal process and paint coating line products; heat treatment furnaces; multi-retort rotary kilns, atmosphere gas generators, and remote maintenance systems; and burner and combustion control equipment, such as gas burners, oil burners, oil/gas burners, oxy burners, heating systems, control and auxiliary equipment, and other systems. The company also film coating and drying equipment, and FPD heat treatment equipment. In addition, it offers air pollution control systems, and kiln and environmental equipment. The company was incorporated in 1945 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

King Jim Co., Ltd. engages in the stationery and electronic products businesses in Japan. It provides a range of files and filing supplies for office and home use. The company also offers electronic products comprising TEPRA, a label printer; and POMERA, a portable digital memo pad. In addition, it is involved in the production of pipe-type binding mechanisms; label printers, filing tools, stationery, storage & organization, office products, and electrical products. The company was formerly known as Meikando Co., Ltd. and changed its name to King Jim Co., Ltd. in 1961. King Jim Co., Ltd. was founded in 1927 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

B&P Co.,Ltd. primarily engages in inkjet printing business in Japan. It is also involved in the production and sale of sales promotion sign displays. In addition, the company engages in proposal and production of large outdoor advertisements and interior goods; custom-made goods; digital signage solution; and proposing and creating AR services. Additionally, it manages online printing site Ink It. B&P Co.,Ltd. was incorporated in 1985 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

ZAOH Company, Ltd. imports and sells machines for anti-pollution and cleaning in Japan. Its products include power sweepers; vacuum cleaners, steam, carpet cleaners, mud and ice removers, and high-pressure cleaners; floor and carpet scrubbers, alkaline ionized water generators, and other products. The company is also involved in the sale of machines and devices for construction, manufacturing, measurement, and transportation; and metalworking oils and related products. The company was incorporated in 1956 and is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

Nippon Carbon Co., Ltd. engages in the manufacture and sale of carbon products in Japan. The company provides carbon fiber products, such as molded heat insulating materials for high temperature furnaces; CARBORON, a soft felt used in heat insulators for high temperature furnaces; C/C Composite, a lightweight carbon material used in heat resistant composite structural members, high temperature furnace materials, trays, and jigs; and CARBEST, a carbon fiber packing material. It offers specialty carbon products, including isotropic graphite; high purity treatment graphite used for manufacturing device components for silicon and compound semiconductors, and optical fibers; carbon products for mechanical components comprising bearings, vane materials, seals, packaging, rings, and mechanical seals; BESCOAT, a silicon carbide coated products; and SC CARBON sliding composites. In addition, the company offers artificial graphite electrodes for use in high power electric arc furnaces; silicon carbide continuous fibers, such as Nicalon, Hi-Nicalon, and Hi-Nicalon Type S for use as composite reinforcement in ceramic, plastic, and other materials; and lithiumion secondary batteries used for cellular and smart phones, NBPC, tablet terminals, power tools, hybrid automobiles, and electric automobiles. Further, it provides impervious graphite RESBON, a corrosion resistant material; NICABEADS, which are surface coated micro bead shape materials; and NICAFILM, a flexible graphite sheet made from natural graphite. Nippon Carbon Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1915 and is headquartered in Chuo, Japan.

IX Knowledge Incorporated provides information technology services in Japan. The company offers IT strategy planning consulting, IT promotion consulting, and BI construction solutions; system development and no-code/low-code development support; system construction, system operation, and outsourcing services; and system verification. It also provides iMPLEO comprehensive quality solution services, including third party verification, system development support, and manual production services; AWS construction and operation support services; RPA/BPR solutions, such as WinActor, Biz Integral, and intra-mart; and Ocean to Table, a seafood traceability service that uses blockchain technology. In addition, the company is involved in civil engineering and construction of related software development; and information system consulting and software development, as well as sales and maintenance. IX Knowledge Incorporated was founded in 1964 and is headquartered in Minato, Japan.

KING Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells textile products. The company offers leather goods; artificial and synthetic leather products; synthetic fiber products; cotton, linen, cellulosic textile products; silk and wool products; pleated products, coats, etc.; and miscellaneous goods, such as bags and shoes, as well as printed and plain pre-dyed clothes. It is involved in wholesaler of women's apparel and fashion goods; and real estate leasing business. It provides its products under the PINORE, JUDY, PRIDE, PRIDE BIS, MORABITO BLANC, MORABITO PREMIER, Pierre Cardin, LETICIA, and LUPUY brands. KING Co., Ltd. was formerly known as King Business Co., Ltd. and changed its name to KING Co., Ltd. in 1978. The company was incorporated in 1948 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Doshisha Co.,Ltd., plans, develops, produces, and sells lifestyle-related products in Japan and Internationally. It operates through Development-oriented Business Model; and Wholesale Business Model. The company provides a wide range of products, such as food and lifestyle products home appliances, lighting, A/V, household goods, interior and storage items, fashion goods and cosmetics, gift, alcohol, and fashion clothing. It also offers watches, jewelries, and bags. The company was founded in 1974 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.

NF Holdings Corporation provides measurement instruments, power supply and power control products, function modules, and customized products in Japan. Its measurement instruments include signal generators, LCR meters, impedance analyzers, AC voltmeters/noise meters, lock-in amplifiers/preamplifiers, filters, measurement systems, and frequency response analyzers; and power supply and power control products comprise AC power sources, power amplifiers, electronic loads, and power test instruments. The company's function modules include filters, amplifiers, oscillators, and phase detectors; and customized products comprise grid-connection testing systems, high-speed bipolar power amplifiers for testing vehicle electronic components, simulated regeneration power supplies, etc. Its products are used in various applications, such as automobiles, digital appliances, electronic devices, fuel cells, solar power, and other clean energy technologies; and nanotechnology, as well as in satellite, rocket, and other aerospace technologies. The company was founded in 1959 and is headquartered in Yokohama, Japan.

Veritas In Silico Inc. engages in the business of small molecule drug discovery and nucleic acid drug discovery targeting mRNA. The company has commercialization agreement with Liverpool ChiroChem for the mRNA-targeted drug discovery and development. Veritas In Silico Inc. was incorporated in 2016 and is based in Shinagawa, Japan.

Matsuoka Corporation engages in planning, manufacturing, and selling apparel products in Japan and internationally. The company is involved in the sewing, wash processing, pleat processing, fabric development, and production of men's and women's casual wear, underwear, and workwear. It also engages in film production and lamination processing; research and development of fabrics and processing technology; and offers trading services. The company was founded in 1956 and is based in Fukuyama, Japan.

Sumitomo Forestry Co., Ltd. engages in forestry business in Japan, the United States, Australia, China, Indonesia, New Zealand, and internationally. It operates through the Timber Building Materials Business; Housing Business; Construction and Real Estate Business; and Resources and Environment Business segments. The Timber Building Materials Business segment purchases, manufactures, processes, and sells timber, including logs, chips, timber, laminated timber, and building materials, consisting of plywood, fiberboard, processed wood composite materials, ceramic building materials, metal building materials, and housing equipment. The Housing Business segment is involved in the contracting, after-sales maintenance, and renovation of construction work for single-family homes and apartment complexes, sales of condominiums, real estate development, leasing, management, buying and selling, and brokerage of real estate, contracting of external structures and landscaping work for housing, urban greening business, CAD, and site surveys. The Construction and Real Estate Business segment sells overseas condominiums, constructs single-family homes, develops housing complexes and commercial complexes, and provides contracting for medium- to large-scale construction work. The Resources and Environment Business segment engages in the renewable energy business, forest resource business. The company was founded in 1691 and is headquartered in Chiyoda, Japan.

Nippon Information Development Co., Ltd. offers information technology solutions in Japan and internationally. It offers mobile solutions, including Android and iOS native applications, and preinstalled applications; a range of technologies for home electronics and IoT, such as cloud-cooperation televisions and game machines; and configurations for terminal applications and various cloud servers. The company also provides a range of embedded technologies comprising ECU development as vehicle infrastructure and automated driving systems of automobiles; medical system technologies for the production of therapeutic and diagnostic apparatus, as well as for the development of hospital information systems; social infrastructure for public wireless domains consisting of airports, trains, fire, and disaster prevention; various technologies for factory and plant control systems; and real-time OS, image processing, communication protocol, and various drivers and middleware systems. In addition, it offers contract and customer management systems for individual and group insurances; non-life insurance systems; mutual aid systems; credit systems; and securities systems. Further, the company provides payroll and benefit systems for civil service; web-based itinerary information validation systems; and logistics systems; and system construction and installation support, network server design/construction, server virtualization environment creation, and cloud migration support services. Additionally, it offers system/network operation maintenance; remote surveillance; IT system operational design improvement; security operational management services; business process outsourcing; and data image entry services, as well as glass live connect, a concierge service solution for online and local stores using SmartGlass; MIEL, a guidance support software for insurance; and Midori Online Accounting System. The company was incorporated in 1967 and is based in Tokyo, Japan.

Lilycolor Co., Ltd. plans, develops, and sells interior products in Japan.It offers wallpaper, curtain, and floor materials under the Lilycolor brand. The company is also involved in the provision of office space and facility interior design, construction, and project management; proposal and sale of furniture, partitions, and office supplies; real estate brokerage; and development, sale, value restoration, and rental and consulting activities for various real estate comprising condominiums, office buildings, commercial facilities, and accommodation and healthcare facilities. It sells its products to interior construction companies and specialty stores, upholstery stores, design offices, housing manufacturers, construction companies, and developers. The company was formerly known as Kawakichi Co., Ltd. and changed its name to Lilycolor Co., Ltd. in 1989. The company was founded in 1907 and is headquartered in Shinjuku, Japan.

Platz Co., Ltd. manufactures and sells care beds, mattresses, and other add-on equipment in Japan. The company's products include nursing care beds, hospital beds, care facility beds, and accessories. The company also offers sales of electric medical care beds and related equipment, and processing and sales of urethane foam. It serves retailers of medical devices and nursing care goods, as well as furniture shops. The company was formerly known as Kyushu Waken ltd. and changed its name to Platz Co., Ltd. in June 1995. Platz Co., Ltd. was incorporated in 1992 and is based in Onojo, Japan.

Hikari Business Form Co., Ltd. provides printing services in Japan.
